7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Lode - build-assembly spine (guard-free cluster, sourced by
20	# rbld0_Lode for the capture kinds and cross-sourced by rbfl0_FoundryLedger for
21	# made-side abjure): the data-driven Cloud Build composer shared by every Lode
22	# capture kind AND by the tool-plane GAR-delete builds (banish/abjure). Takes a
23	# recipe (ordered, pre-resolved step rows) plus an opaque substitutions blob,
24	# composes the Build resource, submits, polls, and decodes a step's
25	# buildStepOutputs slot. Owns NO kind knowledge: the recipe and the substitutions
26	# are data; the only build knobs a body chooses (poll ceiling, run-as identity)
27	# are passed in. The build's run-as serviceAccount is caller-supplied — capture
28	# bodies pass Mason (writer-only, the identity that runs untrusted upstream
29	# bytes), the delete body passes Director (repoAdmin, the only identity that may
30	# delete) — so the spine carries no identity constant of its own; the remaining
31	# spine-owned build constants (TETHER pool, regime build timeout) are
32	# environment, not identity. Built on the rbfcb_ build primitives
33	# (write-script-body, wait-build-completion), which it calls rather than absorbs.
34	#
35	# Sentinel is zrbfc_sentinel, not zrbld_sentinel: the spine binds nothing rbld
36	# beyond its function prefix (it reads only rbfc/rbgd/rbrr/rbdc state), so it is
37	# callable by any rbfc-furnished module — which is what lets abjure ride it from
38	# the separate rbfl process. rbld and rbfl are never co-furnished, so the single
39	# cross-source raises no double-source.
40	#
41	# Contract: RBSCJ "Capture Composition Contract".
42	
43	set -euo pipefail
44	
45	# Multiple inclusion detection — this cluster is multiply-sourced (rbld0_Lode for
46	# the capture kinds, rbfl0_FoundryLedger for made-side abjure), so unlike the
47	# single-entry guard-free clusters it carries its own guard (BCG "the single-guard
48	# rule, and its one exception"). rbld and rbfl are never co-furnished, so the guard
49	# never fires in practice; it is the documented backstop against a future co-furnish.
50	test -z "${ZRBLDS_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rblds multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
51	ZRBLDS_SOURCED=1
52	
53	######################################################################
54	# Build-assembly spine (zrbld_spine_*)
55	
56	# Internal: dispatch-time substitution-coverage check. A capture step reads
57	# automapped _RBGL_* substitution registers; a reference to a key the body's
58	# substitutions blob never defines expands to empty inside the build and corrupts
59	# the capture silently — the one composition fault that is neither guarded in-step
60	# (unlike the /workspace handoff, which the steps test -f for themselves) nor cheap
61	# to surface (it costs the whole cook). The _RBGL_* references in a step body ARE
62	# its substitution requires — no separate declaration to drift from the code — so
63	# this scans the include-expanded body for them on non-comment lines and returns 1
64	# at the first reference absent from the blob's keys, logging the offending
65	# register. Sentinel-free return-1 primitive, like the rbfcb_ build primitives the
66	# dispatch loop already rides; the caller buc_die's with the step identity.
67	#
68	# Coverage is flat by design (RBSCJ "Substitution-coverage check"): substitutions
69	# automap into every step, so there is no cross-step ordering; the /workspace
70	# inter-step channel keeps its own in-step guards and is out of scope.
71	#
72	# This check is also why the build sets options.substitutionOption ALLOW_LOOSE (below):
73	# a python step reads its substitutions as automapped env vars, never as textual
74	# ${_RBGL_*} expansions, so Cloud Build's default MUST_MATCH would reject those keys as
75	# "not matched in the template" the moment a recipe MIXES a python step (env-read subs)
76	# with bash steps (textual-ref subs) — the immure case (python select + bash cp/residency/
77	# vouch). ALLOW_LOOSE lifts that, and this dispatch-time scan is its safe replacement:
78	# it independently catches the inverse fault (a step ${}-referencing a key the blob omits)
79	# that MUST_MATCH would otherwise have caught.
80	#
81	# Args: keys_file expanded_body_file
82	zrbld_spine_validate() {
83	  local -r z_keys_file="${1:?Substitution keys file required}"
84	  local -r z_body_file="${2:?Expanded body file required}"
85	
86	  test -f "${z_keys_file}" || return 1
87	  test -f "${z_body_file}" || return 1
88	
89	  # Keys as newline-bounded text for builtin whole-line membership tests. Empty
90	  # keys (a blob declaring no registers) is legitimate — no test -n.
91	  local -r z_keys=$(<"${z_keys_file}")
92	  local -r z_keys_blob=$'\n'"${z_keys}"$'\n'
93	
94	  # Load the body, then iterate — the file is closed before the scan begins.
95	  local z_lines=()
96	  local z_line=""
97	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
98	    z_lines+=("${z_line}")
99	  done < "${z_body_file}"
100	
101	  local z_i=0
102	  local z_rest=""
103	  local z_ref=""
104	  for z_i in "${!z_lines[@]}"; do
105	    z_line="${z_lines[$z_i]}"
106	
107	    # Full-line comments only — a _RBGL_ token in a leading-# comment is
108	    # documentation, not a read; a trailing inline mention stays conservative.
109	    [[ ! "${z_line}" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] || continue
110	
111	    # Each _RBGL_* token by repeated leftmost match; strip through the match so
112	    # the next token on the line surfaces.
113	    z_rest="${z_line}"
114	    while [[ "${z_rest}" =~ _RBGL_[A-Z0-9_]+ ]]; do
115	      z_ref="${BASH_REMATCH[0]}"
116	      case "${z_keys_blob}" in
117	        *$'\n'"${z_ref}"$'\n'*) ;;
118	        *) buc_log_args "Uncovered substitution register: ${z_ref}"; return 1 ;;
119	      esac
120	      z_rest="${z_rest#*"${z_ref}"}"
121	    done
122	  done
123	
124	  return 0
125	}
126	
127	# Internal: compose, submit, and poll a Lode capture Cloud Build from a recipe
128	# plus an opaque substitutions blob.
129	#
130	# A recipe row is a |-delimited 4-tuple: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint
131	#   - script_path:   absolute path to the step script, pre-resolved by the body
132	#                    (the spine owns no steps-directory knowledge)
133	#   - builder_image: the step's builder image ref (colons allowed — hence the
134	#                    | delimiter, since image tags/digests contain colons)
135	#   - id:            the Cloud Build step id
136	#   - entrypoint:    bash | sh | busybox | python3 — selects the composed shebang
137	#                    line (busybox -> /busybox/sh, the only shell in distroless
138	#                    :debug builder images such as gcrane:debug)
139	#
140	# The substitutions file holds a JSON object the spine slots verbatim into the
141	# Build envelope's `substitutions` field; the spine reads no key from it. The
142	# envelope shape (serviceAccount, options.automapSubstitutions, options.substitutionOption,
143	# options.logging, options.pool, timeout) is the spine's; the run-as identity is caller-supplied
144	# (an SA email the spine composes into the depot-project resource path), and the
145	# environment knobs (TETHER pool, regime timeout) are spine-owned constants.
146	#
147	# Args: token sa_email label poll_ceiling subs_file temp_prefix recipe_row...
148	zrbld_spine_dispatch() {
149	  zrbfc_sentinel
150	
151	  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}";                  shift
152	  local -r z_sa_email="${1:?Service-account email required}"; shift
153	  local -r z_label="${1:?Label required}";                  shift
154	  local -r z_poll_ceiling="${1:?Poll ceiling required}";    shift
155	  local -r z_subs_file="${1:?Substitutions file required}"; shift
156	  local -r z_temp_prefix="${1:?Temp prefix required}";      shift
157	  test "$#" -ge 1 || buc_die "zrbld_spine_dispatch: recipe requires at least one step row"
158	
159	  test -s "${z_subs_file}" || buc_die "Substitutions file missing or empty: ${z_subs_file}"
160	
161	  # Read the substitutions blob's keys once for the dispatch-time coverage check;
162	  # the per-step scan rides the composition loop below, where each step body is
163	  # already include-expanded (zrbld_spine_validate). A JSON object cannot carry
164	  # duplicate keys, so no dedup is needed.
165	  local -r z_keys_file="${z_temp_prefix}subs_keys.txt"
166	  jq -r 'keys[]' "${z_subs_file}" > "${z_keys_file}" \
167	    || buc_die "Failed to read substitution keys from ${z_subs_file}"
168	
169	  buc_step "Composing ${z_label} Cloud Build steps from recipe"
170	  local -r z_steps_file="${z_temp_prefix}steps.json"
171	  echo "[]" > "${z_steps_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize ${z_label} steps JSON"
172	
173	  local z_row=""
174	  local z_script_path=""
175	  local z_builder=""
176	  local z_id=""
177	  local z_entrypoint=""
178	  local z_body_file=""
179	  local z_escaped_file=""
180	  local z_steps_built=""
181	  local z_body=""
182	  local z_shebang=""
183	  for z_row in "$@"; do
184	    IFS='|' read -r z_script_path z_builder z_id z_entrypoint <<<"${z_row}"
185	    test -n "${z_script_path}" || buc_die "Recipe row missing script_path: ${z_row}"
186	    test -n "${z_builder}"     || buc_die "Recipe row missing builder_image: ${z_row}"
187	    test -n "${z_id}"          || buc_die "Recipe row missing id: ${z_row}"
188	    test -f "${z_script_path}" || buc_die "Step script not found: ${z_script_path}"
189	
190	    z_body_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_id}_body.txt"
191	    z_escaped_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_id}_escaped.txt"
192	    z_steps_built="${z_temp_prefix}${z_id}_steps.json"
193	
194	    zrbfc_write_script_body "${z_script_path}" "${z_body_file}" \
195	      || buc_die "Failed to read step script: ${z_script_path}"
196	    zrbfc_expand_includes "${z_body_file}" "${ZRBFC_RBGJS_SNIPPETS_DIR}" \
197	      || buc_die "Failed to expand snippet includes in step: ${z_script_path}"
198	    z_body=$(<"${z_body_file}")
199	    test -n "${z_body}" || buc_die "Empty step script body: ${z_script_path}"
200	
201	    zrbld_spine_validate "${z_keys_file}" "${z_body_file}" \
202	      || buc_die "Recipe step '${z_id}' references a substitution register absent from the composition blob (see transcript)"
203	
204	    case "${z_entrypoint}" in
205	      bash)    z_shebang="#!/bin/bash" ;;
206	      sh)      z_shebang="#!/bin/sh" ;;
207	      busybox) z_shebang="#!/busybox/sh" ;;
208	      python3) z_shebang="#!/usr/bin/env python3" ;;
209	      *)       buc_die "Unknown entrypoint '${z_entrypoint}' in recipe row: ${z_row}" ;;
210	    esac
211	    printf '%s\n%s' "${z_shebang}" "${z_body}" > "${z_escaped_file}" \
212	      || buc_die "Failed to write escaped step body for ${z_id}"
213	
214	    jq \
215	      --arg name "${z_builder}" \
216	      --arg id "${z_id}" \
217	      --rawfile script "${z_escaped_file}" \
218	      '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, script: $script}]' \
219	      "${z_steps_file}" > "${z_steps_built}" \
220	      || buc_die "Failed to append step ${z_id}"
221	    mv "${z_steps_built}" "${z_steps_file}" \
222	      || buc_die "Failed to update steps JSON for ${z_id}"
223	  done
224	
225	  buc_log_args "Composing ${z_label} Build resource JSON"
226	  local -r z_build_file="${z_temp_prefix}build.json"
227	  local -r z_run_as_sa="projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/serviceAccounts/${z_sa_email}"
228	
229	  jq -n \
230	    --slurpfile zjq_steps   "${z_steps_file}" \
231	    --slurpfile zjq_subs    "${z_subs_file}" \
232	    --arg       zjq_sa      "${z_run_as_sa}" \
233	    --arg       zjq_pool    "${RBDC_POOL_TETHER}" \
234	    --arg       zjq_timeout "${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT}" \
235	    '{
236	      steps: $zjq_steps[0],
237	      substitutions: $zjq_subs[0],
238	      serviceAccount: $zjq_sa,
239	      options: {
240	        automapSubstitutions: true,
241	        substitutionOption: "ALLOW_LOOSE",
242	        logging: "CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY",
243	        pool: { name: $zjq_pool }
244	      },
245	      timeout: $zjq_timeout
246	    }' > "${z_build_file}" \
247	    || buc_die "Failed to compose ${z_label} build JSON"
248	
249	  buc_log_args "${z_label} build JSON: ${z_build_file}"
250	
251	  rbrd_check "${z_token}"
252	
253	  buc_step "Submitting ${z_label} Cloud Build"
254	  rbuh_json "POST" "${ZRBFC_GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL}" "${z_token}" \
255	    "lode_build_create" "${z_build_file}"
256	  rbuh_require_ok "${z_label} build submission" "lode_build_create"
257	
258	  local z_build_id
259	  z_build_id=$(rbuh_json_field_capture "lode_build_create" '.metadata.build.id') \
260	    || buc_die "Failed to capture build ID from builds.create response"
261	  test -n "${z_build_id}" || buc_die "Build ID empty in builds.create response"
262	  echo "${z_build_id}" > "${ZRBFC_BUILD_ID_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to persist build ID"
263	
264	  local -r z_console_url="${ZRBFC_CLOUD_QUERY_BASE};region=${RBGD_GCB_REGION}/${z_build_id}?project=${RBGD_GCB_PROJECT_ID}"
265	  buc_info "${z_label} Cloud Build submitted: ${z_build_id}"
266	  buc_link "Click to " "Open build in Cloud Console" "${z_console_url}"
267	
268	  zrbfc_wait_build_completion "${z_poll_ceiling}" "${z_label}"
269	}
270	
271	# Internal: decode the base64 JSON payload a step wrote to its buildStepOutputs
272	# slot into a destination file. The step index is the only generic parameter;
273	# what the decoded JSON means (its member-envelope shape) is the body's
274	# knowledge, not the spine's. Reads ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE (the terminal build
275	# result registered by zrbfc_wait_build_completion).
276	# Args: step_index dest_file
277	zrbld_spine_extract() {
278	  zrbfc_sentinel
279	
280	  local -r z_step_index="${1:?Step index required}"
281	  local -r z_dest_file="${2:?Destination file required}"
282	  local -r z_b64_file="${z_dest_file}.b64"
283	
284	  jq -r ".results.buildStepOutputs[${z_step_index}] // empty" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE}" \
285	    > "${z_b64_file}" || buc_die "Failed to extract buildStepOutputs[${z_step_index}] from build result"
286	  test -s "${z_b64_file}" || buc_die "No buildStepOutputs[${z_step_index}] in build result — step produced no output"
287	
288	  rbgo_base64_decode_file_to_file "${z_b64_file}" "${z_dest_file}" \
289	    || buc_die "Failed to decode buildStepOutputs[${z_step_index}] base64"
290	  test -s "${z_dest_file}" || buc_die "Empty decoded buildStepOutputs[${z_step_index}]"
291	}
292	
293	# eof
294

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 01: Ensconce a base image into a Lode (capture) via gcrane
3	# Builder: gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug (Google-hosted, ambient GAR auth)
4	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT,
5	#                _RBGL_TAG_BOLE, _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX,
6	#                _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA, _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY,
7	#                _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN,
8	#                _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP
9	#
10	# For each non-empty (ORIGIN, STAMP) slot: read the upstream manifest, measure the
11	# canonical digest, gcrane cp into ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp>, then apply the
12	# member tags by gcrane tag (registry-side, no blob round-trip). Author the
13	# provenance envelope and stage it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for
14	# the host capture-file via /builder/outputs/output. gcrane authenticates GAR
15	# ambiently via its google.Keychain (Mason SA, GCE metadata server) — no token.
16	#
17	# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>            (one package = one Lode)
18	# Member tags on that package, all pointing at the base manifest:
19	#   :<TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX><full-hex>   canonical OCI digest (exact cross-Lode dedup)
20	#   :<TAG_BOLE>                      uniform greppable handle
21	#   :<sanitized-origin>-<sha10>      UNSPRUED — name + glance-fingerprint (= legacy enshrine anchor)
22	# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.
23	
24	set -euo pipefail
25	echo "=== Ensconce base images into Lodes ==="
26	
27	# No token fetch: gcrane authenticates GAR ambiently through its google.Keychain
28	# (ADC -> GCE metadata server, the Mason SA). No --creds on any gcrane command.
29	
30	# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
31	# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
32	SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
33	SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
34	SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"
35	SLOT_1_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP}"
36	SLOT_2_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP}"
37	SLOT_3_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP}"
38	
39	# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole build.
40	ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
41	
42	# Stamp roster for step 02 (one line per captured Lode).
43	: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
44	
45	# Initialize result JSON (host reads this back as the capture-file source)
46	RESULT='{'
47	FIRST=true
48	
49	for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
50	  case "${SLOT}" in
51	    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_1_STAMP}" ;;
52	    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_2_STAMP}" ;;
53	    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_3_STAMP}" ;;
54	  esac
55	  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue
56	  test -n "${STAMP}"  || { echo "FATAL: slot ${SLOT} has ORIGIN but no STAMP" >&2; exit 1; }
57	
58	  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} -> ${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP} ---"
59	
60	  # Read the upstream manifest, take the canonical digest, derive the glance
61	  # fingerprint — shared snippet (requires ORIGIN + RAW_FILE; provides SHA + FINGERPRINT).
62	  RAW_FILE="/workspace/ensconce_raw_${SLOT}.json"
63	#@rbgjs_include gcrane-fingerprint
64	
65	  DIGEST_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}${SHA}"
66	  PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
67	
68	  echo "Package: ${PKG}"
69	  echo "Digest:  sha256:${SHA}"
70	  echo "Tags:    ${DIGEST_TAG}, ${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}, ${FINGERPRINT}"
71	
72	  # Collision guard — never silently clobber an existing Lode. The bole handle
73	  # (:rbi_bole) always points at this touchmark's captured image, so read it:
74	  # absent => a fresh touchmark; present => re-use. Re-use is legitimate ONLY when
75	  # it is the identical canonical digest (a Cloud Build retry re-copying the same
76	  # bytes); a different digest under the same touchmark is a clobber and fails loud.
77	  # The check sits immediately before the copy in this same step — atomic with the
78	  # GAR write in the sense the spec requires (no host pre-submit window). EXISTING_SHA
79	  # is computed the same way as SHA (sha256sum of the raw manifest), so the compare is
80	  # apples-to-apples. A manifest-read failure is treated as absent: cloud-side
81	  # auth/network is reliable, and a genuine GAR outage fails the copy below regardless.
82	  EXISTING_RAW="/workspace/ensconce_existing_${SLOT}.json"
83	  if gcrane manifest "${PKG}:${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" > "${EXISTING_RAW}" 2>/dev/null; then
84	    EXISTING_SHA=$(sha256sum "${EXISTING_RAW}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
85	    if [ "${EXISTING_SHA}" = "${SHA}" ]; then
86	      echo "Touchmark ${STAMP} already holds identical digest sha256:${SHA} — idempotent retry, proceeding."
87	    else
88	      echo "FATAL: touchmark collision at ${PKG}" >&2
89	      echo "  existing :${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE} -> sha256:${EXISTING_SHA}" >&2
90	      echo "  refusing to clobber with  -> sha256:${SHA}" >&2
91	      echo "  (banish the Lode first, or ensconce under a fresh touchmark)" >&2
92	      exit 1
93	    fi
94	  else
95	    echo "Touchmark ${STAMP} is fresh (no existing :${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE})."
96	  fi
97	
98	  # Copy upstream into the Lode package under the canonical digest tag. gcrane cp
99	  # copies the whole reference (manifest list and all per-platform manifests for a
100	  # multi-arch index) and preserves the manifest digest byte-for-byte.
101	  gcrane cp "${ORIGIN}" "${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
102	    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane cp failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
103	
104	  # Apply remaining member tags with gcrane tag — a registry-side re-tag of the
105	  # already-copied manifest, no blob round-trip (replaces the GAR->GAR copy).
106	  for MEMBER_TAG in "${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" "${FINGERPRINT}"; do
107	    gcrane tag "${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" "${MEMBER_TAG}" \
108	      || { echo "FATAL: gcrane tag ${MEMBER_TAG} failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
109	  done
110	
111	  echo "Slot ${SLOT} ensconced: ${STAMP}"
112	
113	  # Author the provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and the
114	  # host capture-file). No jq dependency — values are controlled (sanitized origin,
115	  # hex digest, SA email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
116	  # rblv_members[] is the cardinality axis — length 1 for the bole singleton.
117	  ENVELOPE='{'
118	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
119	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_kind\":\"bole\","
120	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
121	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
122	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
123	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
124	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
125	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_signature\":null,"
126	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_members\":[{"
127	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_name\":\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\","
128	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_origin\":\"${ORIGIN}\","
129	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\","
130	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_verification\":\"oci-digest\","
131	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_tags\":[\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\",\"${DIGEST_TAG}\",\"${FINGERPRINT}\"]"
132	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}]}"
133	
134	  # Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact).
135	  printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
136	  echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
137	
138	  # Accumulate host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope).
139	  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
140	    FIRST=false
141	  else
142	    RESULT="${RESULT},"
143	  fi
144	  RESULT="${RESULT}\"rbls_slot_${SLOT}\":{\"rbls_stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"rbls_vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}"
145	done
146	
147	RESULT="${RESULT}}"
148	
149	echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
150	echo "${RESULT}"
151	
152	# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts per-Lode envelope -> capture-file)
153	mkdir -p /builder/outputs
154	printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output
155	
156	echo "=== Ensconce capture step complete ==="
157

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJS token-fetch — OAuth2 access token from the Cloud Build metadata server
3	# (Mason SA). The canonical skopeo-to-GAR authentication (RBSCB): skopeo cannot
4	# use the gcloud credential helper, so the token is fetched in-memory and never
5	# written to /workspace, which carries non-secret data only.
6	#   requires: (none — ambient metadata server)
7	#   provides: TOKEN
8	echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
9	TOKEN_JSON=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
10	  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
11	  || { echo "Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }
12	
13	TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
14	test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }
15

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Lode - reliquary body (guard-free cluster, sourced by rbld0_Lode):
20	#   conclave — convene the build-tool cohort into a Lode (Director credentials)
21	# The reliquary rides the capture-assembly spine (rblds_): this body owns only the
22	# kind-specific data — the conclave recipe (gcrane cohort capture + vouch-push),
23	# the substitutions blob, and the touchmark-fact extract — and composes
24	# them through zrbld_spine_dispatch / zrbld_spine_extract. No build-submission or
25	# step-composition machinery lives here.
26	#
27	# Conclave absorbed the former inscribe pull machinery (rbfli_Inscribe + rbgji01,
28	# now retired): one rbi_ld package holding N member tags (:rbi_<tool>) plus the :rbi_vouch envelope. Both
29	# steps ride the floating gcrane builder (ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER): the tools captured
30	# here ARE the reliquary, so capture cannot bootstrap from one — conclave is the
31	# generation phase the pinning rule permits to run unpinned (RBS0 rbsk_pinning_boundary).
32	
33	set -euo pipefail
34	
35	# Conclave is capture-pure: it writes no consumer config. It hands the captured
36	# touchmark to a later explicit yoke-stamp election (the reliquary cutover, a
37	# separate pace) through two bare single-form chaining facts
38	# (RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK + RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND/RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY). The
39	# provenance envelope lives only in GAR (:rbi_vouch tag, pushed cloud-side by
40	# rbgjl02), never host-side.
41	
42	######################################################################
43	# Internal Helpers (zrbld_*)
44	
45	# Internal: compose the conclave capture recipe (gcrane cohort capture + vouch-push)
46	# and its substitutions blob, then ride the capture spine to submit
47	# and poll. The spine owns the capture-domain build knobs (mason SA, TETHER pool,
48	# regime timeout); this body chooses only the recipe, the substitutions, and the
49	# inscribe-borrowed poll ceiling (the cohort copy needs the larger ceiling — same
50	# work inscribe performs today).
51	# Args: token stamp
52	zrbld_conclave_submit() {
53	  zrbld_sentinel
54	
55	  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
56	  local -r z_stamp="${2:?Stamp required}"
57	
58	  buc_step "Constructing conclave capture recipe"
59	  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
60	  local -r z_gar_path="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
61	
62	  # Recipe rows: script_path|builder_image|id|entrypoint, pre-resolved for the
63	  # spine. Both steps ride the floating gcrane builder (busybox entrypoint —
64	  # gcrane:debug's only shell) — no reliquary bootstrap (conclave IS what captures
65	  # the reliquary tools; generation-tier, the one phase allowed unpinned).
66	  local -r z_recipe=(
67	    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl03-conclave-capture.sh|${ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER}|conclave-capture|busybox"
68	    "${ZRBLD_RBGJL_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjl02-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER}|assemble-push-vouch|busybox"
69	  )
70	
71	  buc_log_args "Composing conclave substitutions blob"
72	  local -r z_subs_file="${ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX}subs.json"
73	  jq -n \
74	    --arg zjq_gar_host     "${z_gar_host}" \
75	    --arg zjq_gar_path     "${z_gar_path}" \
76	    --arg zjq_lodes_root   "${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}" \
77	    --arg zjq_tag_sprue    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}" \
78	    --arg zjq_tag_vouch    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH}" \
79	    --arg zjq_trust_grade  "${RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED}" \
80	    --arg zjq_vouch_schema "${RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA}" \
81	    --arg zjq_acquired_by  "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" \
82	    --arg zjq_stamp        "${z_stamp}" \
83	    '{
84	      _RBGL_GAR_HOST:     $zjq_gar_host,
85	      _RBGL_GAR_PATH:     $zjq_gar_path,
86	      _RBGL_LODES_ROOT:   $zjq_lodes_root,
87	      _RBGL_TAG_SPRUE:    $zjq_tag_sprue,
88	      _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH:    $zjq_tag_vouch,
89	      _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE:  $zjq_trust_grade,
90	      _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA: $zjq_vouch_schema,
91	      _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY:  $zjq_acquired_by,
92	      _RBGL_LODE_STAMP:   $zjq_stamp
93	    }' > "${z_subs_file}" \
94	    || buc_die "Failed to compose conclave substitutions blob"
95	
96	  zrbld_spine_dispatch \
97	    "${z_token}" "${RBGD_MASON_EMAIL}" "Conclave" "${ZRBFC_BUILD_POLL_CEILING_INSCRIBE}" \
98	    "${z_subs_file}" "${ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX}" \
99	    "${z_recipe[@]}"
100	}
101	
102	# Internal: extract the captured touchmark from the completed conclave build and
103	# emit the two bare single-form chaining facts (touchmark value + kind-brand
104	# enum). The capture step (step 0) authors the base64 JSON carrying the
105	# host-minted stamp in slot_1; the vouch-push step writes no output. Conclave
106	# captures exactly one Lode (the cohort is one package), so exactly one slot is
107	# populated. The provenance envelope is NOT read host-side: it lives only in GAR
108	# (rbgjl02 pushed it under :rbi_vouch), so the host hands forward only the
109	# touchmark a consumer needs.
110	zrbld_conclave_extract() {
111	  zrbld_sentinel
112	
113	  buc_step "Extracting capture results from build step outputs"
114	
115	  local -r z_output_file="${ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX}output.json"
116	  zrbld_spine_extract 0 "${z_output_file}"
117	
118	  buc_log_args "Conclave output:"
119	  buc_log_pipe < "${z_output_file}"
120	
121	  local -r z_stamp_file="${ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX}stamp.txt"
122	  jq -r '.rbls_slot_1.rbls_stamp // empty' "${z_output_file}" > "${z_stamp_file}" \
123	    || buc_die "Failed to read reliquary stamp from conclave output"
124	  local -r z_stamp=$(<"${z_stamp_file}")
125	  local -r z_keys_file="${ZRBLD_CONCLAVE_PREFIX}output_keys.txt"
126	  jq -cr 'keys' "${z_output_file}" > "${z_keys_file}" \
127	    || buc_die "Failed to read keys from conclave output"
128	  local -r z_keys=$(<"${z_keys_file}")
129	  test -n "${z_stamp}" || buc_die "Conclave output carried no stamp in rbls_slot_1 (keys present: ${z_keys})"
130	
131	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_TOUCHMARK}" "${z_stamp}" \
132	    || buc_die "Failed to write touchmark fact for ${z_stamp}"
133	  buf_write_fact_single "${RBF_FACT_LODE_BRAND}" "${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY}" \
134	    || buc_die "Failed to write kind-brand fact for ${z_stamp}"
135	  buc_success "Conclave captured Lode ${z_stamp} — touchmark fact emitted (${RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY})"
136	}
137	
138	######################################################################
139	# External Functions (rbld_*)
140	
141	rbld_conclave() {
142	  zrbld_sentinel
143	
144	  buc_doc_brief "Convene the build-tool cohort into one Lode (reliquary kind, rbi_ld capture)"
145	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
146	
147	  # Dirty-tree guard — capture composes its cloud step bodies from the working
148	  # tree; the Lode's provenance envelope must be the product of committed code.
149	  bug_require_clean_tree "conclave"
150	
151	  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
152	  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"
153	
154	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
155	  local z_token=""
156	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
157	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
158	
159	  # Mint the Lode stamp on the host: <kind-letter><YYMMDDHHMMSS>. The host owns
160	  # the stamp so the touchmark is known before the build for the capture-file.
161	  local -r z_stamp="${RBGC_LODE_KIND_RELIQUARY}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:2:6}${BURD_NOW_STAMP:9:6}"
162	
163	  buc_info "Lode: ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
164	
165	  zrbld_conclave_submit "${z_token}" "${z_stamp}"
166	  zrbld_conclave_extract
167	
168	  buc_success "Conclave complete: build-tool cohort -> ${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
169	}
170	
171	# eof
172

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 03: Conclave the build-tool cohort into a Lode (capture) via gcrane
3	# Builder: gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug (Google-hosted, always pullable —
4	#          conclave captures the reliquary tools themselves, so it cannot bootstrap
5	#          from a reliquary; gcrane authenticates GAR ambiently via its google.Keychain
6	#          -> ADC -> the GCE metadata server as the Mason SA, no explicit login. The
7	#          :debug variant carries /busybox/sh for the orchestration. Auth canon: RBSCB)
8	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP,
9	#                _RBGL_TAG_SPRUE, _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA,
10	#                _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY
11	#
12	# Pull each build-tool image from upstream, tag it into ONE GAR package
13	# rbi_ld/<stamp> under the clean member tag :rbi_<tool>, and push. Author the
14	# batch provenance envelope (members[] one per tool — the cardinality axis) and
15	# stage it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for the host capture-file via
16	# /builder/outputs/output. Single-platform (linux/amd64) — tool images run as GCB
17	# steps on amd64 workers; gcrane cp --platform linux/amd64 copies just the amd64
18	# manifest registry->registry (daemonless — no docker daemon, no pull/tag/push).
19	#
20	# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>     (one package = one Lode)
21	# Member tags on that package, each a distinct tool manifest:
22	#   :<TAG_SPRUE><tool>   e.g. rbi_gcloud, rbi_skopeo   (clean scheme — no digest layer)
23	# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.
24	
25	set -euo pipefail
26	echo "=== Conclave build-tool cohort into a Lode ==="
27	
28	STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_STAMP}"
29	test -n "${STAMP}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_LODE_STAMP missing" >&2; exit 1; }
30	
31	PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
32	echo "Lode package: ${PKG}"
33	
34	# Tool image cohort (short-name|upstream-ref) — the authoritative co-versioned set
35	# for GCB step execution (the legacy inscribe manifest rbgji01 is retired). gcloud,
36	# docker, and gcrane are Google-hosted (gcr.io); the rest are third-party. gcrane
37	# rides the :debug variant so the resolved cohort member keeps the /busybox/sh shell
38	# its capture steps need.
39	# The cohort is the heredoc feeding the while-read loop at its `done`, below — this
40	# step runs under /busybox/sh (gcrane:debug's only shell), which has no bash arrays,
41	# so the cohort cannot be expressed as a bash `MANIFEST=( … )` array; a while-read
42	# over a `|`-split heredoc is the POSIX form, matching rbgjl02's stamp loop.
43	
44	# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole cohort.
45	ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
46	
47	# Accumulate the envelope members[] as we capture (one element per tool). No jq
48	# dependency — values are controlled (tool name, upstream ref, hex digest, SA
49	# email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
50	MEMBERS=''
51	MFIRST=true
52	
53	while IFS='|' read -r NAME UPSTREAM; do
54	  test -n "${NAME}" || continue
55	  MEMBER_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_SPRUE}${NAME}"
56	  DEST="${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}"
57	
58	  echo "--- ${NAME}: ${UPSTREAM} -> ${DEST} ---"
59	
60	  # gcrane cp copies registry->registry by digest, daemonless (no docker pull/tag/push).
61	  # --platform linux/amd64 flattens a multi-arch upstream to the SINGLE amd64 manifest.
62	  # This is conclave-ONLY and must not leak to other Lode kinds (bole/wsl keep full-
63	  # fidelity capture): the reliquary cohort is consumed solely as GCB step images on
64	  # amd64 workers, so the other platforms are dead bytes — and a full multi-arch index
65	  # makes the Lode package a parent-index/child-manifest web, where GAR refuses to
66	  # delete a child while its parent index exists (FAILED_PRECONDITION, "referenced by
67	  # parent manifests") and a single packages.delete removes nothing — banish then
68	  # needs multiple convergence rounds instead of one. References: RBSLC, RBSLB, RBSCB.
69	  gcrane --platform linux/amd64 cp "${UPSTREAM}" "${DEST}" \
70	    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane cp failed for ${UPSTREAM} -> ${DEST}" >&2; exit 1; }
71	
72	  # Record the upstream manifest-list digest for the envelope. gcrane digest (no
73	  # --platform) streams the tag's stored index digest (sha256:...) — the same canonical
74	  # value docker's RepoDigests reported, unchanged by the single-platform copy above, so
75	  # recorded digests stay identical to the pre-eviction docker path. CBb_101 applies.
76	  DIGEST=$(gcrane digest "${UPSTREAM}") \
77	    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane digest failed for ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }
78	  test -n "${DIGEST}" || { echo "FATAL: empty digest for ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }
79	
80	  echo "${NAME} captured: ${DEST} (${DIGEST})"
81	
82	  if [ "${MFIRST}" = "true" ]; then MFIRST=false; else MEMBERS="${MEMBERS},"; fi
83	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}{"
84	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"rblv_name\":\"${MEMBER_TAG}\","
85	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"rblv_origin\":\"${UPSTREAM}\","
86	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"rblv_digest\":\"${DIGEST}\","
87	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"rblv_verification\":\"oci-digest\","
88	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"rblv_tags\":[\"${MEMBER_TAG}\"]"
89	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}}"
90	done <<'MANIFEST'
91	gcloud|gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud:latest
92	docker|gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker:latest
93	alpine|docker.io/library/alpine:latest
94	syft|docker.io/anchore/syft:latest
95	binfmt|docker.io/tonistiigi/binfmt:latest
96	skopeo|quay.io/skopeo/stable:latest
97	gcrane|gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug
98	MANIFEST
99	
100	# Author the batch provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and
101	# the host capture-file). rblv_members[] is the cardinality axis — N for the reliquary
102	# cohort, where bole carries 1.
103	ENVELOPE='{'
104	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
105	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_kind\":\"reliquary\","
106	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
107	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
108	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
109	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
110	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
111	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_signature\":null,"
112	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"rblv_members\":[${MEMBERS}]}"
113	
114	# Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact). The
115	# stamps file is the step-02 contract; conclave produces exactly one Lode.
116	printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
117	: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
118	echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
119	
120	# Host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope). One slot —
121	# conclave produces exactly one Lode (the cohort is one package).
122	RESULT="{\"rbls_slot_1\":{\"rbls_stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"rbls_vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}}"
123	
124	echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
125	echo "${RESULT}"
126	
127	# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts the touchmark -> capture-file).
128	mkdir -p /builder/outputs
129	printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output
130	
131	echo "=== Conclave capture step complete ==="
132

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle GCP Constants - Implementation (no printf required)
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	# (Module state remains ZRBGC_* per BCG; external constants use RBGC_*)
25	test -z "${ZRBGC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
26	ZRBGC_SOURCED=1
27	
28	# Tinder constants (pure string literals, no variable expansion — available at source time)
29	# Depot project ID infix between RBRD_CLOUD_PREFIX and RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER, consumed
30	# by rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh's RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID derivation.
31	RBGC_depot_project_infix="d-"
32	
33	######################################################################
34	# Internal Functions (zrbgc_*)
35	
36	zrbgc_kindle() {
37	  test -z "${ZRBGC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgc already kindled"
38	
39	  # Global Resource Naming (Google Cloud global namespace)
40	  # These resources compete in globally-unique namespaces across all of GCP
41	  # Pattern: {prefix}-{type}-{name}-{timestamp} where timestamp is YYMMDDHHMMSS
42	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX="rbwg"
43	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR="p"
44	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_DEPOT="d"
45	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_BUCKET="b"
46	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_FORMAT="+%y%m%d%H%M%S"
47	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN=12
48	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX="[0-9]{${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_LEN}}"
49	
50	  # Global resource validation patterns
51	  # Payor:  rbwg-p-YYMMDDHHMMSS  (timestamp survives — payor is installation-scoped, not depot-scoped)
52	  readonly RBGC_GLOBAL_PAYOR_REGEX="^${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TIMESTAMP_REGEX}$"
53	
54	  # Basic Configuration
55	  readonly RBGC_ADMIN_ROLE="rbw-admin"
56	  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_ROLE="rbw-payor"
57	  readonly RBGC_PAYOR_APP_NAME="Recipe Bottle Payor"
58	
59	  # Timeouts
60	  readonly RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC=90
61	  readonly RBGC_EVENTUAL_CONSISTENCY_SEC=3
62	  # Consecutive 404s rbuh_poll_until_gone requires before declaring a deleted
63	  # resource durably gone. GCP IAM's SA read path is multi-replica eventually-
64	  # consistent: a post-DELETE GET flaps 200<->404 for seconds as replicas
65	  # converge, so a single 404 is not durable proof — a same-name recreate can
66	  # still race a lagging replica's existence preflight. The streak debounces
67	  # that flap; any intervening 200 resets it. Bounded by RBGC_MAX_CONSISTENCY_SEC.
68	  readonly RBGC_GONE_CONFIRM_STREAK=3
69	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_MAX=7
70	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CREATE_RETRY_DELAY_SEC=10
71	
72	  # JWT-bearer consumer-side retry — every RBRA consumer absorbs the post-write
73	  # race where Google's OAuth backend has not yet accepted a freshly-minted SA
74	  # or its key, surfaced as `invalid_grant` paired with either
75	  # `Invalid JWT Signature.` (fresh-key lag) or `Invalid grant: account not
76	  # found` (fresh-SA lag). 90s budget locked by ₣BB pristine-tier contract;
77	  # cadence below.
78	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_BUDGET_SEC=90
79	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC=2
80	  readonly RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_SEC=15
81	
82	  # IAM-grant propagation retry — exponential-backoff budget shared by every
83	  # get-modify-set IAM grant site in rbgi_IAM.sh plus the inline GAR retry in
84	  # rbgg_invest_director. Recognizes three propagation classes against the
85	  # same time budget: (A) forward member-visibility (HTTP 400 "does not
86	  # exist"), (B) backward member-visibility (HTTP 400 "is not deleted"),
87	  # (C) caller-recently-empowered (HTTP 403 from resource-scope IAM caches).
88	  # Class C is time-bounded only — real propagation succeeds within budget,
89	  # real denial waits the budget and fails cleanly. RBSCIP locks the profile.
90	  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC=3
91	  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_MAX_DELAY_SEC=20
92	  readonly RBGC_PROPAGATION_DEADLINE_SEC=420
93	
94	  # HTTP transient-failure retry — bounded retry on curl-network blips
95	  # (connection refused 7, timeout 28, TLS handshake 35, recv failure 56).
96	  # Shared by rbuh_json and the OAuth token-mint POST. Other curl exits
97	  # are configuration-deterministic and fail fast.
98	  readonly RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=3
99	  readonly RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC=3
100	
101	  # docker login daemon->registry transient — the moby/moby#44350 signature.
102	  # docker login's registry-auth client carries a hardcoded, non-configurable
103	  # 15s timeout (moby/registry/auth.go); against a healthy-but-slow GAR auth
104	  # backend it fires prematurely, emitting this Go net/http stdlib string.
105	  # Alone among login/pull/push, login carries no internal retry, so callers
106	  # wrap it (rbgo_docker_login, zrbndb_docker_login) reusing the HTTP retry
107	  # budget above. The string is a Go standard-library invariant, stable across
108	  # docker versions; this is a surveyed-signature allowlist, NOT a catch-all —
109	  # real auth failures emit "unauthorized" and fail fast.
110	  readonly RBGC_DOCKER_LOGIN_TRANSIENT_SIGNATURE='Client.Timeout exceeded while awaiting headers'
111	
112	  # docker login credential-persist failure under headless Cygwin. Docker
113	  # Desktop's docker-credential-wincred backend cannot reach the Windows
114	  # Credential Manager from an sshd session that owns no interactive logon
115	  # (cmdkey /list is empty over SSH), emitting this Win32 string (rc=1) AFTER
116	  # auth has already succeeded — the token mint and the push path are sound;
117	  # only the credential STORE fails. Windows docker also ignores an empty
118	  # credsStore (the CLI still detects wincred), so config alone cannot divert
119	  # the store to the file store. At this Palisade (docker's own credential store,
120	  # source we cannot edit) rbgo_docker_login bends ONCE on this exact signature:
121	  # since auth already succeeded it writes the credential into the base64 file
122	  # store itself (the config.json `auths` map docker push reads directly — the
123	  # form WSL uses natively) and treats login as done. A real auth failure emits
124	  # "unauthorized" and never matches. Stable Win32 system-error message;
125	  # corroborated by docker/cli#4353 and #1263. REMOVE the bend when the
126	  # uncontrolled-Cygwin host gains an interactive Windows logon / working vault.
127	  readonly RBGC_DOCKER_WINCRED_HEADLESS_SIGNATURE='A specified logon session does not exist'

209	  # GAR Lode Layout (fetched-side universal capture — see RBSL)
210	  # One Lode = one GAR package named rbi_ld/<kind-letter><stamp>; that package
211	  # IS the atomic-delete unit (single `packages delete`). Members and provenance
212	  # ride as TAGS within the one package, never as /-path-segments (GAR has no
213	  # subtree delete). Stamp matches the hallmark second-granular form YYMMDDHHMMSS.
214	  #
215	  # Kind letters (one per capture kind; podvm carries two for its quay families):
216	  #   b  bole | r  reliquary | w  wsl | vw  podvm-wsl | vn  podvm-native
217	  # Five kinds, not six: the single-tool kind ('t') was dropped as non-load-
218	  # bearing — it split from reliquary on cardinality alone, has no consumer
219	  # (tool images are only ever consumed as a co-versioned cohort), and the
220	  # package layer already carries 1-vs-N for free (RBSL "Why five kinds, not four").
221	  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_BOLE="b"
222	  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_RELIQUARY="r"
223	  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_WSL="w"
224	  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_PODVM_WSL="vw"
225	  readonly RBGC_LODE_KIND_PODVM_NATIVE="vn"

227	  # Kind-brand enum — the touchmark's kind carried in the host-side single-form
228	  # chaining fact a derived-pull election reads to resolve the member tag. It is
229	  # read as its own fact, NOT parsed from the touchmark's kind-letter prefix
230	  # (the chaining channel is single-form: opaque values, never parsed). Each kind
231	  # adds its brand here with its vertical; the brand string is also the envelope's
232	  # `kind` field, and — for podvm — the operator-typed `immure` family argument.
233	  readonly RBGC_LODE_BRAND_BOLE="bole"
234	  readonly RBGC_LODE_BRAND_RELIQUARY="reliquary"
235	  readonly RBGC_LODE_BRAND_WSL="wsl"
236	  readonly RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_WSL="podvm-wsl"
237	  readonly RBGC_LODE_BRAND_PODVM_NATIVE="podvm-native"

239	  # Member/provenance tags. The rbi_ sprue marks strings from RB's domain:
240	  # RB's authored lexicon (bole, vouch) and RB-measured-from-content values
241	  # (the digest). It does NOT mark foreign-cued strings — the sanitized-origin
242	  # tag is UNSPRUED (origin is a vessel cue), computed at capture, not a constant.
243	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE="rbi_"               # RB reserved tag prefix; member tags compose as <sprue><name>
244	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_BOLE="rbi_bole"            # uniform greppable handle (bole singleton)
245	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_VOUCH="rbi_vouch"          # one-per-Lode provenance envelope
246	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX="rbi_sha256-"  # canonical OCI digest tag: rbi_sha256-<full-hex>
247	  # reliquary cohort members carry the clean scheme :<sprue><tool> (e.g. rbi_skopeo)
248	  # — no digest/fingerprint layer; the tool name is RB-authored lexicon, so sprued.
249	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TAG_ROOTFS="rbi_rootfs"        # wsl singleton: the opaque rootfs blob member (RB-authored, sprued)
250	
251	  # Provenance envelope (:rbi_vouch) — two honest trust grades, declared per Lode.
252	  # bole captures the durable-upstream grade; podvm-* carries the recorded grade.
253	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TRUST_VERIFIED="verified-against-published"
254	  readonly RBGC_LODE_TRUST_RECORDED="recorded-at-acquisition"
255	  # Schema bumped to rbld-vouch-2 with the rblv_ sprue migration (ACGm_108, first application).
256	  # Pre-MVP: no back-compat; every author writes rblv_ keys, augur reads rblv_ ONLY.
257	  readonly RBGC_LODE_VOUCH_SCHEMA="rbld-vouch-2"    # unsigned, schema-versioned, rblv_ sprue
258	
259	  # wsl-kind acquisition convention — NOT a resolved coordinate (see RBSLU). Per
260	  # the no-FQIN premise, intent stays declarative and the pipeline computes the
261	  # coordinate: underpin takes the substrate version as ARGUMENTS (release + point,
262	  # e.g. `24.04 4`); the host _capture function assembles the tarball URL from this
263	  # path-convention template, and the cloud step DISCOVERS the checksum at capture
264	  # — it fetches Canonical's published, GPG-signed SHA256SUMS, verifies the
265	  # signature against the pinned signing-key fingerprint below, then verifies the
266	  # rootfs bytes. No full URL and no digest are pinned here; advancing the version
267	  # is a different argument, not a constant edit. The template bets on Canonical's
268	  # cdimage path scheme staying stable — a fail-loud bet (a 404 / missing sums-line
269	  # dies clean). printf args: (release, release.point, arch).
270	  #
271	  # Palisade note (RBSLU): the paddock named cloud-images.ubuntu.com/wsl/, but that
272	  # path retired its checksummed-tarball publication — noble/ now ships only
273	  # .manifest files and a Store-delivered .wsl. Ubuntu Base (cdimage) is the
274	  # genuinely-checksummed, GPG-signed, wsl --import-shaped Canonical equivalent.
275	  # Acquisition-only this heat, so the distro flavor is not load-bearing; the
276	  # WSL-specific seed is a consumption-time re-pin (wsl --import deferred — see
277	  # RBSLU, paddock Heat nature). The signing fingerprint is per-source: re-pin it
278	  # with any source change. Retire the note if the wsl/ tarball publication returns.
279	  readonly RBGC_LODE_WSL_URL_TEMPLATE="https://cdimage.ubuntu.com/ubuntu-base/releases/%s/release/ubuntu-base-%s-base-%s.tar.gz"
280	  readonly RBGC_LODE_WSL_ARCH_DEFAULT="amd64"
281	  # Ubuntu CD Image Automatic Signing Key (2012, RSA4096), cdimage@ubuntu.com —
282	  # signs cdimage SHA256SUMS.gpg. The trust anchor: tiny, stable, auditable. The
283	  # cloud step fetches the key BY this fingerprint into a clean keyring, so the
284	  # keyserver is never trusted — only a signature from exactly this key passes.
285	  readonly RBGC_LODE_WSL_SIGNING_FPR="843938DF228D22F7B3742BC0D94AA3F0EFE21092"
286	
287	  # podvm-kind acquisition convention — NOT resolved coordinates (see RBSLI). Per
288	  # the no-FQIN premise, intent stays declarative and the pipeline resolves leaf
289	  # digests at capture: immure takes the quay FAMILY and the podman VERSION as
290	  # arguments (e.g. `podvm-wsl 5.6`); the cloud select step reads the family's
291	  # multi-arch OCI index at that version and picks the curated {disktype × arch}
292	  # leaves below from the index child DESCRIPTOR's platform.architecture +
293	  # annotations.disktype — never the layer filename, which is unreliable (the 5.6
294	  # wsl x86_64 leaf is titled `5.0-rootfs-amd64.tar.zst`; memo-20260608 §3.4). No
295	  # digest is pinned; advancing the version is a different argument, not a constant
296	  # edit. Trust grade is recorded-at-acquisition — quay rotates podvm out within
297	  # days and publishes no durable checksum, so RB attests only the digest captured.
298	  #
299	  # Two quay families, one verb (immure spans both via the family argument). The
300	  # disktype leaves carry the ALT arch spelling (x86_64/aarch64), not the OCI
301	  # amd64/arm64 the plain container children use — selection keys on that spelling.
302	  readonly RBGC_LODE_PODVM_FAMILY_WSL="quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl"
303	  readonly RBGC_LODE_PODVM_FAMILY_NATIVE="quay.io/podman/machine-os"
304	  # Curated leaf selection per family — declarative `disktype:arch` rows the select
305	  # step matches against index child descriptors (alt arch spelling: x86_64/aarch64).
306	  # Member tag composes as :<sprue><disktype>-<arch> (e.g. rbi_wsl-x86_64);
307	  # disktype+arch are RB-selected from the index, so sprued.
308	  # WSL family: 2-leaf set (both wsl-disktype leaves). The podvm-wsl fixture proves
309	  # this end-to-end with the multi-member machinery and per-member-jettison path.
310	  readonly RBGC_LODE_PODVM_WSL_SELECTION="wsl:x86_64 wsl:aarch64"
311	  # Native family: full 8-leaf curation — {applehv, hyperv, qemu, wsl} × {x86_64, aarch64}.
312	  # Sourced from memo-20260608 §5 (the machine-os index carries exactly these 8 disktype
313	  # children at the 5.6 observation point; the 2 plain-container children are EXCLUDED
314	  # by disktype-key selection). This pace (lode-podvm-platform-fanout) lands full
315	  # curation; the "FOLLOWING pace" deferral is retired. Native full-curation is gated
316	  # one-time (not a recurring service fixture) — see the podvm-lifecycle fixture comment.
317	  readonly RBGC_LODE_PODVM_NATIVE_SELECTION="applehv:x86_64 applehv:aarch64 hyperv:x86_64 hyperv:aarch64 qemu:x86_64 qemu:aarch64 wsl:x86_64 wsl:aarch64"

1	#!/bin/sh
2	# RBGJR Step 01: In-pool reliquary preflight — HEAD-check tool images and base anchors
3	# Builder: alpine (from reliquary)
4	# Substitutions: _RBGR_GAR_HOST, _RBGR_GAR_PATH, _RBGR_LODES_ROOT, _RBGR_TAG_SPRUE,
5	#                _RBGR_RELIQUARY,
6	#                _RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_1, _RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_2, _RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_3
7	#
8	# Defense-in-depth: every Cloud Build job validates reliquary GAR-presence
9	# from the worker pool's vantage before expensive work runs. Catches divergence
10	# the host-side preflight cannot — airgap pool private-VPC routing, IAM split
11	# between Director and Mason service accounts, time skew between submission and
12	# execution.
13	#
14	# Coverage:
15	#   - Reliquary tool images (5 of 6): gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo.
16	#     Alpine (this step's own image) is implicitly validated by Cloud Build
17	#     pulling it for step execution; failure to pull alpine produces Cloud
18	#     Build's own "image not pullable" error before this script runs.
19	#   - Base anchor locators (slots 1/2/3 if non-empty). Conjure populates
20	#     these; bind and graft pass them through empty.
21	#
22	# Failure mode: structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message naming each
23	# missed item. Operator remediation: re-conclave the reliquary, re-yoke the
24	# vessel, re-ordain the hallmark.
25	#
26	# Auth: Mason SA via Cloud Build metadata server. HEAD requests via busybox
27	# wget (no curl in alpine by default; apk add not viable on airgap pools).
28	# wget exit code is the binary signal — 0 for HTTP 2xx, non-zero otherwise.
29	
30	# POSIX sh (busybox ash on alpine): pipefail is non-portable and not load-bearing
31	# here — the one pipe (token extract) is validated by the test -n guard below.
32	set -eu
33	
34	test -n "${_RBGR_GAR_HOST}"   || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_GAR_HOST missing"   >&2; exit 1; }
35	test -n "${_RBGR_GAR_PATH}"   || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_GAR_PATH missing"   >&2; exit 1; }
36	test -n "${_RBGR_LODES_ROOT}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_LODES_ROOT missing" >&2; exit 1; }
37	test -n "${_RBGR_TAG_SPRUE}"  || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_TAG_SPRUE missing"  >&2; exit 1; }
38	test -n "${_RBGR_RELIQUARY}"  || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_RELIQUARY missing"  >&2; exit 1; }
39	
40	echo "=== In-pool reliquary preflight ==="
41	echo "Reliquary: ${_RBGR_RELIQUARY}"
42	echo "GAR base:  ${_RBGR_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGR_GAR_PATH}"
43	
44	echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
45	TOKEN_JSON=$(wget -q -O - \
46	  --header="Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
47	  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
48	  || { echo "FATAL: Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }
49	
50	TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
51	test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "FATAL: Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }
52	
53	ACCEPT_MTYPES="application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json,application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json,application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json,application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json"
54	
55	REGISTRY_API_BASE="https://${_RBGR_GAR_HOST}/v2/${_RBGR_GAR_PATH}"
56	
57	MISSES=""
58	
59	# Tool list mirrors the conclave cohort manifest (rbgjl03) minus alpine.
60	# Conclave Lode layout: ONE package <LODES_ROOT>/<RELIQUARY> carrying the cohort
61	# as sprued member tags; each tool is the manifest TAG :<TAG_SPRUE><NAME>.
62	for TOOL in gcloud docker syft binfmt skopeo; do
63	  PKG_PATH="${_RBGR_LODES_ROOT}/${_RBGR_RELIQUARY}"
64	  MEMBER_TAG="${_RBGR_TAG_SPRUE}${TOOL}"
65	  URL="${REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${PKG_PATH}/manifests/${MEMBER_TAG}"
66	
67	  echo "--- HEAD ${PKG_PATH}:${MEMBER_TAG} ---"
68	  if wget -q -O /dev/null \
69	       --header="Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" \
70	       --header="Accept: ${ACCEPT_MTYPES}" \
71	       "${URL}"; then
72	    echo "  PRESENT: reliquary-tool ${TOOL}"
73	  else
74	    echo "  MISSING: reliquary-tool ${TOOL}"
75	    MISSES="${MISSES} reliquary-tool:${TOOL}"
76	  fi
77	done
78	
79	# Base slots — empty for non-conjure modes; conjure populates from RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR.
80	# Locator format: <package-path>:<tag>; package path is itself prefixed by the
81	# Lode namespace already (e.g. rbi_ld/<touchmark>:rbi_bole).
82	for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
83	  case "${SLOT}" in
84	    1) LOCATOR="${_RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_1}" ;;
85	    2) LOCATOR="${_RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_2}" ;;
86	    3) LOCATOR="${_RBGR_BASE_LOCATOR_3}" ;;
87	  esac
88	  test -n "${LOCATOR}" || continue
89	
90	  case "${LOCATOR}" in
91	    *:*) : ;;
92	    *)   echo "FATAL: invalid base locator format (expected package-path:tag) slot ${SLOT}: ${LOCATOR}" >&2; exit 1 ;;
93	  esac
94	  PKG_PATH="${LOCATOR%:*}"
95	  TAG="${LOCATOR##*:}"
96	  test -n "${PKG_PATH}" || { echo "FATAL: empty package path in locator slot ${SLOT}: ${LOCATOR}" >&2; exit 1; }
97	  test -n "${TAG}"      || { echo "FATAL: empty tag in locator slot ${SLOT}: ${LOCATOR}" >&2; exit 1; }
98	  URL="${REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${PKG_PATH}/manifests/${TAG}"
99	
100	  echo "--- HEAD ${PKG_PATH}:${TAG} (base slot ${SLOT}) ---"
101	  if wget -q -O /dev/null \
102	       --header="Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" \
103	       --header="Accept: ${ACCEPT_MTYPES}" \
104	       "${URL}"; then
105	    echo "  PRESENT: base-anchor slot ${SLOT}"
106	  else
107	    echo "  MISSING: base-anchor slot ${SLOT}"
108	    MISSES="${MISSES} base-anchor:${LOCATOR}"
109	  fi
110	done
111	
112	if [ -z "${MISSES}" ]; then
113	  echo "=== Reliquary preflight passed — pool sees all tools and base anchors ==="
114	  exit 0
115	fi
116	
117	echo "" >&2
118	echo "=== Reliquary preflight FAILED — this pool can't see: ===" >&2
119	for MISS in ${MISSES}; do
120	  echo "  «${MISS}»" >&2
121	done
122	echo "" >&2
123	echo "Defense-in-depth: every Cloud Build verifies reliquary GAR-presence from" >&2
124	echo "the worker pool's vantage. The Director's host-side preflight passed, but" >&2
125	echo "the Mason pool cannot reach one or more required artifacts. Likely causes:" >&2
126	echo "  - airgap pool private-VPC routing missing for these artifacts" >&2
127	echo "  - IAM split between Director and Mason service accounts" >&2
128	echo "  - time skew between host-side preflight and pool-side build execution" >&2
129	echo "" >&2
130	echo "Remediation: re-conclave reliquary, re-yoke vessel, re-ordain hallmark." >&2
131	exit 1
132

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Foundry Ledger - yoke cluster (guard-free, sourced by rbflk_):
20	# validate a reliquary stamp against GAR, then rewrite RBRV_RELIQUARY across every
21	# vessel's rbrv.env (Director credentials).
22	
23	set -euo pipefail
24	
25	######################################################################
26	# Yoke (rbfl_*)
27	
28	rbfl_yoke() {
29	  zrbfl_sentinel
30	
31	  local -r z_stamp="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
32	
33	  buc_doc_brief "Yoke a reliquary touchmark into every vessel's rbrv.env — pre-validate the conclave Lode once against GAR, then rewrite RBRV_RELIQUARY across all vessels under \${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}"
34	  buc_doc_param "touchmark" "Reliquary Lode touchmark (e.g., r260327172456)"
35	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
36	
37	  test -n "${z_stamp}" || buc_die "Reliquary touchmark required (param1)"
38	
39	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
40	  local z_token=""
41	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
42	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
43	
44	  buc_step "Validating reliquary Lode: ${z_stamp}"
45	  local -r z_pkg="${RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${z_stamp}"
46	  local -r z_pkg_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"
47	  local -r z_tags_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_pkg_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1000"
48	  local -r z_list_infix="rbfl_yoke_list"
49	
50	  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_tags_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_list_infix}"
51	  rbuh_require_ok "List conclave Lode tags" "${z_list_infix}"
52	
53	  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_list_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
54	  local -r z_present_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_yoke_present.txt"
55	
56	  jq -r '.tags[]?.name | sub(".*/tags/"; "")' "${z_resp_file}" > "${z_present_file}" \
57	    || buc_die "Failed to extract conclave Lode member tags"
58	
59	  local z_present=()
60	  local z_line=""
61	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
62	    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
63	    z_present+=("${z_line}")
64	  done < "${z_present_file}"
65	
66	  # Expected cohort members as SPRUED tags (:rbi_<tool>) — compose RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE
67	  # onto each bare tool seed, matching the conclave member-tag scheme (rbgjl03). The
68	  # seeds stay inputs; the membership check is always against the sprued member tag.
69	  local -r z_expected=(
70	    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD}"
71	    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER}"
72	    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE}"
73	    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT}"
74	    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT}"
75	    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO}"
76	    "${RBGC_LODE_TAG_SPRUE}${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCRANE}"
77	  )
78	
79	  local z_missing=""
80	  local z_tool=""
81	  for z_tool in "${z_expected[@]}"; do
82	    local z_found=0
83	    local z_i=""
84	    for z_i in "${!z_present[@]}"; do
85	      test "${z_present[$z_i]}" = "${z_tool}" || continue
86	      z_found=1
87	      break
88	    done
89	    case "${z_found}" in
90	      1) ;;
91	      *) z_missing="${z_missing}${z_missing:+, }${z_tool}" ;;
92	    esac
93	  done
94	
95	  # Derive count and roster from the expected array so a cohort change (a tool
96	  # added or evicted) keeps these messages accurate without a hand-edit.
97	  local z_roster=""
98	  local z_r=""
99	  for z_r in "${z_expected[@]}"; do z_roster="${z_roster}${z_roster:+, }${z_r}"; done
100	
101	  # Render the conclave-tabtarget hint from its colophon home (RBZ_CONCLAVE_RELIQUARY)
102	  # via the tt yawp — never a hardcoded tt/<colophon>.<frontispiece>.sh literal, which
103	  # rots on rename. buc_die resolves the diastema-wrapped yelp through buyf_format_yawp.
104	  buyy_tt_yawp "${RBZ_CONCLAVE_RELIQUARY}"; local -r z_conclave_tt="${z_buym_yelp}"
105	  test -z "${z_missing}" || buc_die "Reliquary Lode '${z_stamp}' incomplete in Depot — expected ${#z_expected[@]} tool member tags on ${z_pkg}; missing: ${z_missing}. Re-run ${z_conclave_tt} to capture a fresh reliquary Lode, or verify the touchmark spelling."
106	  buc_info "Reliquary Lode valid — all ${#z_expected[@]} tool member tags present (${z_roster})"
107	
108	  buc_step "Yoking ${z_stamp} into all vessels under ${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}"
109	
110	  local z_written=()
111	  local z_vessel_dir=""
112	  local z_rbrv_file=""
113	  local z_sigil=""
114	  local z_tmp_file=""
115	  local z_rbrv_lines=()
116	  local z_rbrv_line=""
117	  local z_wrote=0
118	  local z_j=""
119	
120	  for z_vessel_dir in "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}"/*/; do
121	    test -d "${z_vessel_dir}" || continue
122	    z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir%/}/rbrv.env"
123	    test -f "${z_rbrv_file}" || continue
124	    z_sigil="${z_vessel_dir%/}"
125	    z_sigil="${z_sigil##*/}"
126	
127	    z_rbrv_lines=()
128	    while IFS= read -r z_rbrv_line || test -n "${z_rbrv_line}"; do
129	      z_rbrv_lines+=("${z_rbrv_line}")
130	    done < "${z_rbrv_file}"
131	
132	    z_tmp_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_yoke_${z_sigil}_rbrv.env.new"
133	    : > "${z_tmp_file}" \
134	      || buc_die "Failed to create ${z_tmp_file} (yoking ${z_sigil}; already wrote: ${z_written[*]:-(none)})"
135	
136	    z_wrote=0
137	    for z_j in "${!z_rbrv_lines[@]}"; do
138	      case "${z_rbrv_lines[$z_j]}" in
139	        RBRV_RELIQUARY=*)
140	          printf 'RBRV_RELIQUARY=%s\n' "${z_stamp}" >> "${z_tmp_file}" \
141	            || buc_die "Failed to write RBRV_RELIQUARY for ${z_sigil} (already wrote: ${z_written[*]:-(none)})"
142	          z_wrote=1
143	          ;;
144	        *)
145	          printf '%s\n' "${z_rbrv_lines[$z_j]}" >> "${z_tmp_file}" \
146	            || buc_die "Failed to write line for ${z_sigil} (already wrote: ${z_written[*]:-(none)})"
147	          ;;
148	      esac
149	    done
150	
151	    case "${z_wrote}" in
152	      1) ;;
153	      *) printf '\n# Tool Image Reliquary\nRBRV_RELIQUARY=%s\n' "${z_stamp}" >> "${z_tmp_file}" \
154	           || buc_die "Failed to append RBRV_RELIQUARY for ${z_sigil} (already wrote: ${z_written[*]:-(none)})" ;;
155	    esac
156	
157	    mv "${z_tmp_file}" "${z_rbrv_file}" \
158	      || buc_die "Failed to finalize ${z_rbrv_file} (yoking ${z_sigil}; already wrote: ${z_written[*]:-(none)})"
159	
160	    z_written+=("${z_sigil}")
161	    buc_log_args "Yoked ${z_sigil}"
162	  done
163	
164	  test "${#z_written[@]}" -gt 0 \
165	    || buc_die "No vessels found under ${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR} — nothing yoked"
166	
167	  buc_success "Yoked ${#z_written[@]} vessel(s) to reliquary ${z_stamp}"
168	  buc_info "Vessels: ${z_written[*]}"
169	  buc_info "Commit the rbrv.env changes with your usual git workflow."
170	  buc_info "Reminder: the reliquary tool images are now linked, but vessel images must be rebuilt (ordain) to pick up the new tool versions."
171	}
172	
173	# eof
174

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Foundry Ledger - inventory cluster (guard-free, sourced by rbflk_):
20	# tally hallmark health, rekon a hallmark/reliquary subtree, and audit hallmarks
21	# and reliquaries (Retriever for tally; Director for the rest).
22	
23	set -euo pipefail
24	
25	######################################################################
26	# Inventory (rbfl_*)
27	
28	rbfl_tally() {
29	  zrbfl_sentinel
30	
31	  buc_doc_brief "Tally hallmarks with health status (vouched / pending / incomplete)"
32	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
33	
34	  buc_step "Authenticating as Retriever"
35	  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" \
36	    || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
37	  local z_token=""
38	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") \
39	    || buc_die "Failed to get Retriever OAuth token"
40	
41	  buc_step "Enumerating hallmarks under ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/"
42	  zrbfc_list_packages_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}"
43	
44	  if ! test -s "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"; then
45	    buc_info "No hallmarks found under ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/"
46	    buc_success "Tally complete — 0 hallmarks"
47	    return 0
48	  fi
49	
50	  # Load-then-iterate. A synthetic sentinel element appended to the array
51	  # lets the final hallmark flush through the same boundary branch as every
52	  # intermediate one (single flush site).
53	  local z_lines=()
54	  local z_line=""
55	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
56	    z_lines+=("${z_line}")
57	  done < "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"
58	  z_lines+=("__SENTINEL__ __SENTINEL__")
59	
60	  echo ""
61	  printf "  %-30s  %-11s  %s\n" "HALLMARK" "HEALTH" "BASENAMES"
62	  printf "  %-30s  %-11s  %s\n" "------------------------------" "-----------" "---------"
63	
64	  # State machine over <hallmark> <basename> pairs (file was sorted by the
65	  # capture helper). Vessel is no longer encoded in the GAR path —
66	  # restoration via about/vouch metadata is AAL territory.
67	  local z_prev_h="" z_prev_bns=""
68	  local z_prev_img=0 z_prev_abt=0 z_prev_vch=0
69	  local z_count=0 z_vouched_n=0 z_pending_n=0 z_incomplete_n=0
70	  local z_i="" z_h="" z_b="" z_health=""
71	
72	  for z_i in "${!z_lines[@]}"; do
73	    z_line="${z_lines[$z_i]}"
74	    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
75	
76	    z_h="${z_line%% *}"
77	    z_b="${z_line#* }"
78	    test -n "${z_h}" || continue
79	    test -n "${z_b}" || continue
80	
81	    if test "${z_h}" != "${z_prev_h}"; then
82	      if test -n "${z_prev_h}"; then
83	        if test "${z_prev_img}" = "1" \
84	          && test "${z_prev_abt}" = "1" \
85	          && test "${z_prev_vch}" = "1"; then
86	          z_health="vouched"
87	          z_vouched_n=$(( z_vouched_n + 1 ))
88	        elif test "${z_prev_img}" = "1" \
89	          && test "${z_prev_abt}" = "1"; then
90	          z_health="pending"
91	          z_pending_n=$(( z_pending_n + 1 ))
92	        else
93	          z_health="incomplete"
94	          z_incomplete_n=$(( z_incomplete_n + 1 ))
95	        fi
96	        printf "  %-30s  %-11s  %s\n" "${z_prev_h}" "${z_health}" "${z_prev_bns}"
97	        z_count=$(( z_count + 1 ))
98	      fi
99	
100	      case "${z_h}" in
101	        __SENTINEL__) break ;;
102	      esac
103	
104	      z_prev_h="${z_h}"
105	      z_prev_bns=""
106	      z_prev_img=0
107	      z_prev_abt=0
108	      z_prev_vch=0
109	    fi
110	
111	    z_prev_bns="${z_prev_bns}${z_prev_bns:+ }${z_b}"
112	    case "${z_b}" in
113	      "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}") z_prev_img=1 ;;
114	      "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT}") z_prev_abt=1 ;;
115	      "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}") z_prev_vch=1 ;;
116	    esac
117	  done
118	
119	  echo ""
120	  buc_info "Total hallmarks: ${z_count}  (vouched: ${z_vouched_n}, pending: ${z_pending_n}, incomplete: ${z_incomplete_n})"
121	
122	  case "${z_pending_n}" in
123	    0) ;;
124	    *) buc_info "To vouch pending hallmarks:"
125	       buc_tabtarget "rbw-fV"
126	       ;;
127	  esac
128	
129	  case "${z_incomplete_n}" in
130	    0) ;;
131	    *) buc_info "To abjure incomplete hallmarks:"
132	       buc_tabtarget "rbw-fA"
133	       ;;
134	  esac
135	
136	  buc_success "Tally complete"
137	}
138	
139	rbfl_rekon_hallmark() {
140	  zrbfl_sentinel
141	
142	  local -r z_hallmark="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
143	
144	  buc_doc_brief "List ark basenames present under a hallmark's GAR subtree"
145	  buc_doc_param "hallmark" "Hallmark identifier"
146	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
147	
148	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Usage: rbw-irh <hallmark>"
149	
150	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
151	  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
152	    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
153	  local z_token=""
154	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
155	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
156	
157	  buc_step "Enumerating arks under ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/"
158	  zrbfc_list_packages_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}"
159	
160	  # Filter the full hallmark enumeration to rows for this hallmark.
161	  local z_found=""
162	  local z_line=""
163	  local z_h=""
164	  local z_b=""
165	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
166	    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
167	    z_h="${z_line%% *}"
168	    z_b="${z_line#* }"
169	    if test "${z_h}" = "${z_hallmark}"; then
170	      z_found="${z_found}${z_found:+ }${z_b}"
171	    fi
172	  done < "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"
173	
174	  test -n "${z_found}" || buc_die "Hallmark not found: ${z_hallmark}"
175	
176	  echo ""
177	  printf "  %-10s  %-6s  %s\n" "BASENAME" "EXISTS" "PACKAGE-PATH"
178	  printf "  %-10s  %-6s  %s\n" "----------" "------" "------------"
179	
180	  local z_canon=""
181	  local z_mark=""
182	  local z_path=""
183	  for z_canon in \
184	    "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}" \
185	    "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT}" \
186	    "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}" \
187	    "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ATTEST}" \
188	    "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_POUCH}" \
189	    "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS}"; do
190	    z_mark="no"
191	    case " ${z_found} " in
192	      *" ${z_canon} "*) z_mark="yes" ;;
193	    esac
194	    if test "${z_mark}" = "yes"; then
195	      z_path="${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}/${z_canon}"
196	    else
197	      z_path="(absent)"
198	    fi
199	    printf "  %-10s  %-6s  %s\n" "${z_canon}" "${z_mark}" "${z_path}"
200	  done
201	
202	  echo ""
203	  buc_success "Rekon complete for ${z_hallmark}"
204	}
205	
206	rbfl_audit_hallmarks() {
207	  zrbfl_sentinel
208	
209	  buc_doc_brief "Audit hallmarks — list all hallmark identifiers in registry"
210	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
211	
212	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
213	  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
214	    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
215	  local z_token=""
216	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
217	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
218	
219	  buc_step "Enumerating hallmarks under ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/"
220	  zrbfc_list_packages_capture "${z_token}" "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}"
221	
222	  if ! test -s "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"; then
223	    buc_info "No hallmarks found under ${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/"
224	    buc_success "Audit complete — 0 hallmarks"
225	    return 0
226	  fi
227	
228	  echo ""
229	  printf "  %s\n" "HALLMARK"
230	  printf "  %s\n" "------------------------------"
231	
232	  local z_count=0
233	  local z_prev=""
234	  local z_line=""
235	  local z_h=""
236	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
237	    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
238	    z_h="${z_line%% *}"
239	    test -n "${z_h}" || continue
240	    if test "${z_h}" != "${z_prev}"; then
241	      printf "  %s\n" "${z_h}"
242	      buf_write_fact_multi "${z_h}" "${RBCC_fact_ext_audit_hallmark}" "${z_h}"
243	      z_count=$(( z_count + 1 ))
244	      z_prev="${z_h}"
245	    fi
246	  done < "${ZRBFC_PACKAGE_LIST_FILE}"
247	
248	  echo ""
249	  buc_info "Total hallmarks: ${z_count}"
250	  buc_success "Audit complete"
251	}
