253	######################################################################
254	# Raw-path list (rbfl_*) — the type-blind maintenance backdoor (rbw-il)
255	
256	# Generic, envelope-independent enumeration of GAR by raw path, narrowing
257	# iteratively: no path -> top namespaces; a prefix -> its children; a full
258	# package -> its tags and versions. Makes no subtree or kind assumption — it
259	# reads the whole registry and narrows in-process — so it tolerates half-
260	# deleted debris, legacy artifacts, and any future namespace with no new verb.
261	rbfl_list() {
262	  zrbfl_sentinel
263	
264	  local -r z_path="${BUZ_FOLIO:-}"
265	
266	  buc_doc_brief "List GAR contents by iterative path narrowing — the type-blind raw maintenance layer"
267	  buc_doc_oparm "path" "Raw GAR path. Omit for the top namespaces; a prefix lists its children; a full package lists its tags and versions. A ref carrying :tag or @sha256: is an image — use rbw-iw / rbw-iJ."
268	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
269	
270	  # Disambiguation (total rule — GAR's deletable leaves are exactly tags and
271	  # versions): a ref carrying :tag or @sha256: is an image, not a path. list
272	  # walks paths only; acting on an image is wrest (rbw-iw) / jettison (rbw-iJ).
273	  case "${z_path}" in
274	    *@*|*:*) buc_die "'${z_path}' is an image ref, not a path — use rbw-iw (wrest) or rbw-iJ (jettison)" ;;
275	  esac
276	
277	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
278	  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" \
279	    || buc_die "Director credential not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
280	  local z_token=""
281	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
282	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
283	
284	  # One enumeration of every package (decoded names, slashes restored, sorted).
285	  buc_step "Enumerating GAR packages"
286	  local -r z_list_infix="rbfl_list_pkgs"
287	  local -r z_list_url="${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages?pageSize=1000"
288	  rbuh_json "GET" "${z_list_url}" "${z_token}" "${z_list_infix}"
289	  rbuh_require_ok "List GAR packages" "${z_list_infix}"
290	  local -r z_resp_file="${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_list_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}"
291	
292	  local -r z_pkgs_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_list_packages.txt"
293	  jq -r '
294	    .packages[]?.name
295	    | sub("^.*/packages/"; "")
296	    | gsub("%2F"; "/")
297	  ' "${z_resp_file}" | sort > "${z_pkgs_file}" \
298	    || buc_die "Failed to extract GAR package list"
299	
300	  # Leaf grain: the path names a package exactly -> list its tags and versions.
301	  if test -n "${z_path}"; then
302	    local z_pl=""
303	    while IFS= read -r z_pl || test -n "${z_pl}"; do
304	      if test "${z_pl}" = "${z_path}"; then
305	        zrbfl_list_leaf "${z_token}" "${z_path}"
306	        return 0
307	      fi
308	    done < "${z_pkgs_file}"
309	  fi
310	
311	  # Branch grain: emit the distinct next path-segment beneath the prefix
312	  # (empty path -> the top namespaces).
313	  local z_prefix=""
314	  test -z "${z_path}" || z_prefix="${z_path}/"
315	
316	  local -r z_kids_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_list_children.txt"
317	  jq -r --arg prefix "${z_prefix}" '
318	    .packages[]?.name
319	    | sub("^.*/packages/"; "")
320	    | gsub("%2F"; "/")
321	    | select(startswith($prefix))
322	    | ltrimstr($prefix)
323	    | split("/")[0]
324	    | select(length > 0)
325	  ' "${z_resp_file}" | sort -u > "${z_kids_file}" \
326	    || buc_die "Failed to derive child path segments"
327	
328	  if ! test -s "${z_kids_file}"; then
329	    test -n "${z_path}" || buc_die "No packages found in registry"
330	    buc_die "No path or package matches: ${z_path}"
331	  fi
332	
333	  echo ""
334	  if test -z "${z_path}"; then
335	    printf "  %s\n" "TOP NAMESPACES"
336	  else
337	    printf "  CHILDREN OF  %s\n" "${z_path}"
338	  fi
339	  printf "  %s\n" "------------------------------"
340	  local z_seg=""
341	  while IFS= read -r z_seg || test -n "${z_seg}"; do
342	    test -n "${z_seg}" || continue
343	    if test -z "${z_path}"; then
344	      printf "  %s\n" "${z_seg}"
345	    else
346	      printf "  %s/%s\n" "${z_path}" "${z_seg}"
347	    fi
348	  done < "${z_kids_file}"
349	
350	  echo ""
351	  buc_success "List complete"
352	}
353	
354	# Leaf display for rbfl_list: a full package's tags and versions, each annotated
355	# with the ref form that wrest/jettison consume (tag -> :tag, version -> @digest).
356	zrbfl_list_leaf() {
357	  zrbfl_sentinel
358	
359	  local -r z_token="${1:?Token required}"
360	  local -r z_pkg="${2:?Package path required}"
361	  local -r z_encoded="${z_pkg//\//%2F}"
362	
363	  buc_step "Listing tags and versions of ${z_pkg}"
364	
365	  local -r z_tags_infix="rbfl_list_tags"
366	  rbuh_json "GET" \
367	    "${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_encoded}/tags?pageSize=1000" \
368	    "${z_token}" "${z_tags_infix}"
369	  rbuh_require_ok "List tags for ${z_pkg}" "${z_tags_infix}"
370	  local -r z_tags_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_leaf_tags.txt"
371	  jq -r '.tags[]?.name | sub("^.*/tags/"; "")' \
372	    "${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_tags_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}" | sort > "${z_tags_file}" \
373	    || buc_die "Failed to extract tags for ${z_pkg}"
374	
375	  local -r z_vers_infix="rbfl_list_vers"
376	  rbuh_json "GET" \
377	    "${ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE}/${ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE}/packages/${z_encoded}/versions?pageSize=1000" \
378	    "${z_token}" "${z_vers_infix}"
379	  rbuh_require_ok "List versions for ${z_pkg}" "${z_vers_infix}"
380	  local -r z_vers_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfl_leaf_vers.txt"
381	  jq -r '.versions[]?.name | sub("^.*/versions/"; "")' \
382	    "${ZRBUH_PREFIX}${z_vers_infix}${ZRBUH_POSTFIX_JSON}" | sort > "${z_vers_file}" \
383	    || buc_die "Failed to extract versions for ${z_pkg}"
384	
385	  echo ""
386	  printf "  PACKAGE  %s\n" "${z_pkg}"
387	  echo ""
388	  printf "  %s\n" "TAGS  (act:  rbw-iw ${z_pkg}:<tag>   rbw-iJ ${z_pkg}:<tag>)"
389	  printf "  %s\n" "------------------------------"
390	  if test -s "${z_tags_file}"; then
391	    local z_tag=""
392	    while IFS= read -r z_tag || test -n "${z_tag}"; do
393	      test -n "${z_tag}" || continue
394	      printf "    %s\n" "${z_tag}"
395	    done < "${z_tags_file}"
396	  else
397	    printf "    (none)\n"
398	  fi
399	
400	  echo ""
401	  printf "  %s\n" "VERSIONS  (act:  rbw-iJ ${z_pkg}@<version>)"
402	  printf "  %s\n" "------------------------------"
403	  if test -s "${z_vers_file}"; then
404	    local z_ver=""
405	    while IFS= read -r z_ver || test -n "${z_ver}"; do
406	      test -n "${z_ver}" || continue
407	      printf "    %s\n" "${z_ver}"
408	    done < "${z_vers_file}"
409	  else
410	    printf "    (none)\n"
411	  fi
412	
413	  echo ""
414	  buc_success "List complete — ${z_pkg}"
415	}

26	rbho_director_first_build() {
27	  zrbho_sentinel
28	
29	  buc_doc_brief "${RBHO_TRACK_FIRST_BUILD} — conclave, conjure, tour, summon, abjure"
30	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
31	
32	  local -r z_vessel="rbev-sentry-deb-tether"
33	
34	  local z_has_director=0
35	  local z_secrets_dir=""
36	  if test -f "${RBCC_rbrr_file}"; then
37	    z_secrets_dir=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBCC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_SECRETS_DIR") || z_secrets_dir=""
38	  fi
39	  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}" && \
40	     test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_account_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; then
41	    z_has_director=1
42	  fi
43	
44	  local z_has_depot=0
45	  if test -f "${RBCC_rbrd_file}"; then
46	    local z_line=""
47	    while IFS= read -r z_line; do
48	      case "${z_line}" in RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER=?*) z_has_depot=1; break ;; esac
49	    done < "${RBCC_rbrd_file}"
50	  fi
51	
52	  local z_conjure_summoned=0
53	  if command -v docker >/dev/null 2>&1; then
54	    local z_project_id=""
55	    local z_region=""
56	    if test -f "${RBCC_rbrd_file}"; then
57	      z_project_id=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBCC_rbrd_file}" "RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER") || z_project_id=""
58	      z_region=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBCC_rbrd_file}" "RBRD_GCP_REGION") || z_region=""
59	    fi
60	    if test -n "${z_region}" && test -n "${z_project_id}"; then
61	      local -r z_gar_prefix="${z_region}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${z_project_id}/"
62	      local -r z_df_out="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_IMAGES_PREFIX}5_repotag.txt"
63	      local -r z_df_err="${ZRBHO_DOCKER_STDERR_PREFIX}7_repotag.txt"
64	      if docker images --format "{{.Repository}}:{{.Tag}}" \
65	           > "${z_df_out}" 2>"${z_df_err}"; then
66	        local z_line=""
67	        while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
68	          case "${z_line}" in
69	            "${z_gar_prefix}"*"${z_vessel}:c"[0-9]*) z_conjure_summoned=1; break ;;
70	          esac
71	        done < "${z_df_out}"
72	      fi
73	    fi
74	  fi
75	
76	  buyy_link_yawp "${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}" "Vessel" "${z_vessel}"; local -r z_lk_vessel_name="${z_buym_yelp}"
77	
78	  buh_section "${RBHO_TRACK_FIRST_BUILD}"
79	  buh_e
80	  buh_line "This track walks you through the complete ${RBYC_CONJURE} lifecycle:"
81	  buh_line "provision the builder toolchain, ${RBYC_ORDAIN} your first ${RBYC_VESSEL} via"
82	  buh_line "Cloud Build, inspect the result, pull it locally, and clean up."
83	  buh_e
84	  buh_line "You will build ${z_lk_vessel_name} — the same ${RBYC_SENTRY} you"
85	  buh_line "already know from the ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} track, but this time built by"
86	  buh_line "Google Cloud Build with full SLSA ${RBYC_PROVENANCE}."
87	  buh_e
88	
89	  buh_line "Prerequisites:"
90	  buh_e
91	  if test "${z_has_director}" = "1"; then
92	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}${RBYC_DIRECTOR} credential installed"
93	  else
94	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}${RBYC_DIRECTOR} credential missing — run:"
95	    buh_tt "      " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_CRED_DIRECTOR}"
96	  fi
97	  if test "${z_has_depot}" = "1"; then
98	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}${RBYC_DEPOT} configured (RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER populated)"
99	  else
100	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}${RBYC_DEPOT} not configured — the ${RBYC_PAYOR} must establish the ${RBYC_DEPOT}:"
101	    buh_tt "      " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_PAYOR_HB}"
102	  fi
103	  buh_e
104	
105	  if test "${z_has_director}" = "0" || test "${z_has_depot}" = "0"; then
106	    buh_error "Complete the prerequisites above before continuing."
107	    buh_e
108	    buh_tt "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
109	    buh_e
110	    return 0
111	  fi
112	
113	  buh_line "Configure this handbook session:"
114	  buh_e
115	  buh_code "   export ${RBYC_HANDBOOK_VESSEL_NAME}=${z_vessel}"
116	  buh_e
117	  buh_line "This sets the ${RBYC_VESSEL} you will build throughout the track."
118	  buh_line "The remaining steps reference it by name."
119	  buh_e
120	
121	  buh_step_style "Step " " — "
122	
123	  buh_step1 "Conclave the ${RBYC_RELIQUARY}"
124	  buh_e
125	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_RELIQUARY} is a set of six builder tool images (gcloud,"
126	  buh_line "docker, alpine, syft, binfmt, skopeo) that Cloud Build uses during"
127	  buh_line "${RBYC_VESSEL} construction. Without it, the ${RBYC_CONJURE} preflight"
128	  buh_line "check fails."
129	  buh_e
130	  buh_line "Think of it as installing the toolchain before your first build."
131	  buh_line "This is a one-time operation — once conclaved, the ${RBYC_RELIQUARY}"
132	  buh_line "stays in the ${RBYC_DEPOT} until you choose to refresh it."
133	  buh_e
134	  buh_line "Periodically re-conclave to pick up newer tool versions. All ${RBYC_VESSELS}"
135	  buh_line "share the same ${RBYC_RELIQUARY} — one conclave updates the toolchain"
136	  buh_line "for every build."
137	  buh_e
138	  buh_line "If you have uncommitted changes, commit them first — capture and"
139	  buh_line "build commands refuse on a dirty working tree, so every artifact"
140	  buh_line "traces to a committed state."
141	  buh_e
142	  buh_line "Conclave:"
143	  buh_e
144	  buh_tt "   " "${RBZ_CONCLAVE_RELIQUARY}"
145	  buh_e
146	  buh_line "This captures six tool images from upstream into the ${RBYC_DEPOT}"
147	  buh_line "GAR namespace. Takes 2-5 minutes depending on network speed."
148	  buh_e
149	  buyy_cmd_yawp "r260324193326";                   local -r z_ds_example="${z_buym_yelp}"
150	  buh_line "When conclave completes, it prints a ${RBYC_RELIQUARY} datestamp"
151	  buh_line "(e.g., ${z_ds_example}). Yoke wildcard-fans that stamp into every"
152	  buh_line "${RBYC_VESSEL}'s ${RBYC_REGIME} under \${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR} in one pass —"
153	  buh_line "there is no per-vessel argument:"
154	  buh_e
155	  buh_tt "   " "${RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY}" "" " <stamp>"
156	  buh_e
157	  buh_line "Yoke validates the stamp once against GAR, then rewrites RBRV_RELIQUARY"
158	  buh_line "in every ${RBYC_VESSEL}'s rbrv.env. Commit the changes — ${RBYC_ORDAIN}"
159	  buh_line "refuses to build from an uncommitted tree."
160	  buh_e
161	  buh_line "Reminder: yoking links the new ${RBYC_RELIQUARY} into the ${RBYC_VESSEL}"
162	  buh_line "${RBYC_REGIME}, but existing ${RBYC_VESSEL} images still embed the old tool"
163	  buh_line "versions until you rebuild them via ${RBYC_ORDAIN}."
164	  buh_e
165	
166	  buh_step1 "${RBYC_CONJURE} the ${RBYC_SENTRY}"
167	  buh_e
168	  buh_line "${RBYC_CONJURE} is the build mode where Cloud Build constructs a"
169	  buh_line "${RBYC_VESSEL} image from the project's Dockerfile and build context."
170	  buh_e
171	  buh_line "${RBYC_ORDAIN} is the command that triggers the full pipeline —"
172	  buh_line "it reads the ${RBYC_VESSEL} ${RBYC_RBRV} ${RBYC_REGIME} to determine the mode"
173	  buh_line "(${RBYC_CONJURE}, ${RBYC_BIND}, or ${RBYC_GRAFT}) and acts accordingly:"
174	  buh_e
175	  buh_tt "   " "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "" " ${RBYC_HANDBOOK_VESSEL_REF}"
176	  buh_e
177	  buh_line "This builds on the ${RBYC_TETHERED} pool — Cloud Build has"
178	  buh_line "public internet access and pulls base images from upstream"
179	  buh_line "registries during the build. (The ${RBYC_AIRGAP} track removes"
180	  buh_line "that dependency.)"
181	  buh_e
182	  buh_line "The pipeline:"
183	  buh_e
184	  buh_line "  1. The host mints a ${RBYC_HALLMARK} — a timestamped tag"
185	  buh_line "     identifying this build"
186	  buh_line "  2. A ${RBYC_POUCH} (build context archive) is pushed to GAR"
187	  buh_line "  3. Cloud Build constructs the image across platforms"
188	  buh_line "  4. SLSA ${RBYC_PROVENANCE} is generated per platform digest"
189	  buh_line "  5. ${RBYC_VOUCH} verifies the ${RBYC_PROVENANCE} chain"
190	  buh_e
191	  buh_warn "Wall-clock: ~15-20 minutes for a 3-platform build."
192	  buh_line "The command blocks until Cloud Build finishes. Use the time"
193	  buh_line "to read ahead — the next steps explain what to look for."
194	  buh_e
195	
196	  buh_step1 "Capture the ${RBYC_HALLMARK}"
197	  buh_e
198	  buh_line "When ${RBYC_ORDAIN} completes, it writes the ${RBYC_HALLMARK}"
199	  buh_line "to the ${RBYC_OUTPUT} directory — a fixed-path staging area"
200	  buh_line "that each ${RBYC_TABTARGET} clears and recreates on entry."
201	  buh_line "Read the ${RBYC_HALLMARK} from the fact file and export it so"
202	  buh_line "you can copy-paste the commands in the remaining steps:"
203	  buh_e
204	  buh_code "   export ${RBYC_HANDBOOK_HALLMARK_NAME}=\$(cat ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK})"
205	  buh_e
206	
207	  buh_step1 "Tour the ${RBYC_HALLMARK} artifacts"
208	  buh_e
209	  buh_line "Every ${RBYC_CONJURED} ${RBYC_HALLMARK} produces a set of arks"
210	  buh_line "in GAR under ${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS}/{hallmark}/. Each basename serves a specific role:"
211	  buh_e
212	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS}/{hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_POUCH}:{hallmark}";        local -r z_sfx_pouch="${z_buym_yelp}"
213	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS}/{hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}:{hallmark}";        local -r z_sfx_image="${z_buym_yelp}"
214	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS}/{hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ATTEST}:{hallmark}-{arch}"; local -r z_sfx_attest="${z_buym_yelp}"
215	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS}/{hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT}:{hallmark}";        local -r z_sfx_about="${z_buym_yelp}"
216	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS}/{hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:{hallmark}";        local -r z_sfx_vouch="${z_buym_yelp}"
217	  buyy_cmd_yawp "${RBGC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS}/{hallmark}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS}:{hallmark}";        local -r z_sfx_diags="${z_buym_yelp}"
218	  buh_line "   ${z_sfx_pouch}"
219	  buh_line "      A FROM SCRATCH OCI image pushed from host to GAR before"
220	  buh_line "      the build. Contains the Dockerfile, scripts, and"
221	  buh_line "      configuration Cloud Build needs. Identical for ${RBYC_TETHERED}"
222	  buh_line "      and ${RBYC_AIRGAP} builds — the pool determines network"
223	  buh_line "      access, not the ${RBYC_POUCH}."

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJS gcrane-fingerprint — read an upstream image's raw manifest, take its
3	# canonical sha256 digest, and derive the sanitized-origin glance fingerprint.
4	# The canonical digest is the sha256 of the RAW manifest bytes (what every tool
5	# reports); the fingerprint is the sanitized origin (`:` and `/` become `-`) plus
6	# the first 10 hex of that digest — the legacy enshrine anchor form. `gcrane
7	# manifest` streams the registry's stored manifest bytes verbatim (no
8	# re-serialization), so the digest is byte-identical to skopeo's `inspect --raw`
9	# and to the canonical OCI digest. The first GAR copy is kind-specific (its
10	# destination tag is itself digest-derived) and stays in the step.
11	#   requires: ORIGIN    upstream image ref (e.g. docker.io/library/x:tag)
12	#             RAW_FILE  /workspace path to write the raw manifest into
13	#   provides: SHA          64-hex canonical digest (no algorithm prefix)
14	#             FINGERPRINT  "<sanitized-origin>-<first-10-hex>"
15	gcrane manifest "${ORIGIN}" > "${RAW_FILE}" \
16	  || { echo "FATAL: Failed to read upstream manifest: ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }
17	
18	# sha256sum (busybox coreutils, not openssl) — runs inside the gcrane:debug builder.
19	SHA=$(sha256sum "${RAW_FILE}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
20	test -n "${SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: Empty digest for ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }
21	
22	# Sanitize origin (: and / become -), append first 10 hex chars (legacy anchor form).
23	FINGERPRINT="$(printf '%s' "${ORIGIN}" | tr ':/' '--')-${SHA:0:10}"
24

14	//
15	// Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
16	//
17	// RBTDRU — cupel: BCG command-dependency static-analysis fixture.
18	//
19	// A cupel is the assay vessel in which base metals are driven off and the
20	// noble metal remains. This fixture drives off command-position tokens that
21	// violate the Bash Console Guide's Command Dependency Discipline, leaving a
22	// corpus whose external-command surface is exactly the declared dependency
23	// floor.
24	//
25	// BCG (section "Command Dependency Discipline") is the single source of truth:
26	//   - POSIX floor   — irreducible external commands, no builtin replacement
27	//   - Declared deps — the RBS0 Dependency Inventory (bash/git/curl/jq/docker/
28	//                     openssl/ssh/scp/ssh-keygen + developer/specialized tools)
29	//   - Eviction table — commands with builtin/declared replacements; enforced,
30	//                     failing with BCG's prescribed replacement
31	//
32	// Algorithm — two-pass, function-aware, with asymmetric scope. Pass 1 collects
33	// every locally-defined function name across the WHOLE corpus (all kits, minus
34	// dead ABANDONED code) so that cross-kit and sourced names resolve; pass 2 lints
35	// only the release kit roots, flagging each command-position token not in {bash
36	// builtins, local functions, POSIX floor, declared deps} and failing-with-
37	// replacement on the eviction table. Soundness rests on the corpus already being
38	// shellcheck-clean (enforced at release qualification and marshal-zero), so a
39	// command-position lexer suffices — no
40	// full shell parser.
41	//
42	// Corpus scope — only the release-relevant kit roots (Tools/buk, Tools/rbk) are
43	// linted. These are the kits that ship in the recipe-bottle consumer release and
44	// are authored under BCG; other kits under Tools/ are separate products never
45	// written to the discipline, and holding them to it would surface noise, not
46	// defects. A kit adopts the discipline by being added to ZRBTDRU_KIT_ROOTS —
47	// opt-in, never by default. Within the lint target, ABANDONED* and FUTURE*
48	// directories are excluded (dead / not-yet-live); the pass-1 function universe
49	// excludes only ABANDONED* (FUTURE* code is present and sourceable, so its
50	// definitions stay visible).
51	//
52	// Two execution-environment domains, partitioned by path:
53	//   - Kit-bash   — strict BCG. Eviction table enforced; unknown commands fail.
54	//   - GCB-bash   — Google Cloud Build job scripts under any Tools/rbk/rbgj*
55	//                  directory. Looser: they run in the cloud-sdk image where the
56	//                  evicted commands and gcloud are present, so evictions are not
57	//                  enforced and the GCB-extra allowlist is added.
58	//
59	// Known lexer limits (accepted per the fixture's design — the corpus is
60	// shellcheck-clean and the discovery run surfaces residue for triage):
61	//   - Command substitutions nested inside double-quoted strings are not scanned.
62	//   - The `;&` / `;;&` case fall-through operators are treated as a plain `;`.
63

65	use std::path::{
66	    Path,
67	    PathBuf,
68	};
69	
70	use crate::case;
71	use crate::rbtdre_engine::{
72	    rbtdre_Case,

75	    rbtdre_Verdict,
76	};
77	use crate::rbtdrm_manifest::RBTDRM_FIXTURE_CUPEL;
78	
79	// ── Corpus location ─────────────────────────────────────────
80	
81	/// Repo-relative directory holding all kit trees, walked per release kit root.
82	pub(crate) const ZRBTDRU_TOOLS_SUBDIR: &str = "Tools";
83	
84	/// Release-relevant kit roots under Tools/, each walked recursively. Only these
85	/// kits ship in the recipe-bottle consumer release and are authored under BCG,
86	/// so only these are held to the discipline. Names are directory basenames under
87	/// Tools/; adding one opts that kit into the lint deliberately.
88	pub(crate) const ZRBTDRU_KIT_ROOTS: &[&str] = &["buk", "rbk"];
89	
90	/// Extension (no dot) selecting bash files from the corpus walk.
91	pub(crate) const ZRBTDRU_SH_EXT: &str = "sh";
92	
93	/// Directory-name prefix marking the Google Cloud Build job family. Any bash
94	/// under a `Tools/rbk/rbgj*` directory is GCB-bash. Partitioning by this prefix
95	/// — rather than a hardcoded directory list — keeps the partition drift-proof
96	/// as new rbgj* job groups are added.
97	pub(crate) const ZRBTDRU_GCB_DIR_PREFIX: &str = "rbgj";
98	
99	/// Dead-code directory prefixes excluded from the function-visibility universe.
100	/// `ABANDONED*` is retained for reference but unbuilt and unsourceable, so its

19	# Recipe Bottle Handbook Onboarding - Director Bind Mode
20	#
21	# Teaches bind mode using rbev-bottle-plantuml: pin an upstream image
22	# by digest into GAR, no Dockerfile, no build context, no SLSA. The
23	# pluml Crucible deliberately mixes a kludged Sentry with a bound
24	# Bottle — two ordain modes cohabiting in one Crucible is the
25	# expected shape, not a defect.
26	
27	set -euo pipefail
28	
29	test -z "${ZRBHODB_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
30	ZRBHODB_SOURCED=1
31	
32	rbho_director_bind() {
33	  zrbho_sentinel
34	
35	  buc_doc_brief "${RBHO_TRACK_BIND} — pin upstream image by digest, mode-mixture pluml Crucible"
36	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
37	
38	  local -r z_moniker="pluml"
39	  local -r z_vessel="rbev-bottle-plantuml"
40	  local -r z_vessel_rbrv="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_vessel}/rbrv.env"
41	  local -r z_pluml_rbrn="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${z_moniker}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
42	
43	  local z_has_director=0
44	  local z_secrets_dir=""
45	  if test -f "${RBCC_rbrr_file}"; then
46	    z_secrets_dir=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${RBCC_rbrr_file}" "RBRR_SECRETS_DIR") || z_secrets_dir=""
47	  fi
48	  if test -n "${z_secrets_dir}" && \
49	     test -f "${z_secrets_dir}/${RBCC_account_director}/${RBCC_rbra_file}"; then
50	    z_has_director=1
51	  fi
52	
53	  local z_has_depot=0
54	  if test -f "${RBCC_rbrd_file}"; then
55	    local z_line=""
56	    while IFS= read -r z_line; do
57	      case "${z_line}" in RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER=?*) z_has_depot=1; break ;; esac
58	    done < "${RBCC_rbrd_file}"
59	  fi
60	
61	  local z_sentry_ready=0
62	  if test -f "${z_pluml_rbrn}"; then
63	    local z_sentry_hallmark=""
64	    z_sentry_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_pluml_rbrn}" "RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK") || z_sentry_hallmark=""
65	    case "${z_sentry_hallmark}" in
66	      ""|PENDING-*) ;;
67	      *) z_sentry_ready=1 ;;
68	    esac
69	  fi
70	
71	  local z_bottle_bound=0
72	  if test -f "${z_pluml_rbrn}"; then
73	    local z_bottle_hallmark=""
74	    z_bottle_hallmark=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_pluml_rbrn}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK") || z_bottle_hallmark=""
75	    case "${z_bottle_hallmark}" in
76	      ""|PENDING-*) ;;
77	      *) z_bottle_bound=1 ;;
78	    esac
79	  fi
80	
81	  local z_vessel_yoked=0
82	  if test -f "${z_vessel_rbrv}"; then
83	    local z_vessel_stamp=""
84	    z_vessel_stamp=$(zrbho_po_extract_capture "${z_vessel_rbrv}" "RBRV_RELIQUARY") || z_vessel_stamp=""
85	    test -n "${z_vessel_stamp}" && z_vessel_yoked=1
86	  fi
87	
88	  buyy_link_yawp "${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}" "Vessel"    "${z_vessel}";   local -r z_lk_vessel="${z_buym_yelp}"
89	  buyy_link_yawp "${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}" "Nameplate" "${z_moniker}";  local -r z_lk_pluml="${z_buym_yelp}"
90	
91	  buh_section "${RBHO_TRACK_BIND} — Pin Upstream by Digest"
92	  buh_e
93	  buh_line "${RBHO_TRACK_FIRST_BUILD} taught ${RBYC_CONJURE} — Cloud Build constructs"
94	  buh_line "the ${RBYC_VESSEL} image from the project's Dockerfile, and SLSA"
95	  buh_line "${RBYC_PROVENANCE} attests the build chain. ${RBYC_BIND} is the simplest"
96	  buh_line "${RBYC_ORDAIN} mode: no Dockerfile, no build context, no image build —"
97	  buh_line "Cloud Build runs skopeo to copy the digest-pinned upstream image into"
98	  buh_line "your ${RBYC_DEPOT}, then about/vouch records what was mirrored."
99	  buh_e
100	  buh_line "This track binds ${z_lk_vessel} — the PlantUML server image. PlantUML"
101	  buh_line "renders diagrams, but its Docker Hub source could phone home if run"
102	  buh_line "naked. ${RBYC_BIND} pins it by content hash; the ${RBYC_SENTRY} blocks all"
103	  buh_line "egress at runtime. You get the tool without the risk."
104	  buh_e
105	
106	  buh_line "Prerequisites:"
107	  buh_e
108	  if test "${z_has_director}" = "1"; then
109	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}${RBYC_DIRECTOR} credential installed"
110	  else
111	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}${RBYC_DIRECTOR} credential missing — run:"
112	    buh_tt "      " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_CRED_DIRECTOR}"
113	  fi
114	  if test "${z_has_depot}" = "1"; then
115	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_YES}${RBYC_DEPOT} configured (RBRD_DEPOT_MONIKER populated)"
116	  else
117	    buh_line "${RBYC_PROBE_NO}${RBYC_DEPOT} not configured — the ${RBYC_PAYOR} must establish the ${RBYC_DEPOT}:"
118	    buh_tt "      " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_PAYOR_HB}"
119	  fi
120	  buh_e
121	
122	  if test "${z_has_director}" = "0" || test "${z_has_depot}" = "0"; then
123	    buh_error "Complete the prerequisites above before continuing."
124	    buh_e
125	    buh_tt "Return to start: " "${RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE}"
126	    buh_e
127	    return 0
128	  fi
129	
130	  buh_section "Mode mixture — name it explicitly"
131	  buh_e
132	  buh_line "The ${z_lk_pluml} ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} in this track pairs a ${RBYC_KLUDGE_D}"
133	  buh_line "(or ${RBYC_CONJURED}) ${RBYC_SENTRY} with a ${RBYC_BIND}-pinned PlantUML ${RBYC_BOTTLE}."
134	  buh_line "Two different ${RBYC_ORDAIN} modes cohabiting in one ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE} is the"
135	  buh_line "expected shape, not a defect — each ${RBYC_HALLMARK} carries its own"
136	  buh_line "${RBYC_VOUCH} verdict, and a ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} can pin ${RBYC_HALLMARKS} of"
137	  buh_line "different modes without conflict."
138	  buh_e
139	  buh_line "The ${RBYC_SENTRY} enforces network policy regardless of how it was"
140	  buh_line "built; the policy is what's load-bearing, not the build mode behind"
141	  buh_line "it. The bind lesson is about what happens to the ${RBYC_BOTTLE} image."
142	  buh_e
143	
144	  buh_step_style "Step " " — "
145	
146	  buh_step1 "Ready a ${RBYC_SENTRY} for the ${z_lk_pluml} ${RBYC_CRUCIBLE}"
147	  buh_e
148	  buh_line "Before you can ${RBYC_CHARGE} ${z_lk_pluml}, the ${RBYC_NAMEPLATE} needs a"
149	  buh_line "${RBYC_SENTRY} ${RBYC_HALLMARK}. Two paths reach the same outcome:"
150	  buh_e
151	  buh_line "  Primary path — ${RBYC_KLUDGE} the ${RBYC_SENTRY} locally. Same"
152	  buh_line "  pattern you met in ${RBHO_TRACK_FIRST_CRUCIBLE}: the host builds the"
153	  buh_line "  image and writes the resulting ${RBYC_HALLMARK} into the ${z_lk_pluml}"

/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSIJ-image_jettison.adoc:71:.. NOTE: V2 DELETE-by-tag has producer-side asymmetric semantics. For single-platform pushes (`docker push`), the tag points to a single image manifest; DELETE removes it, the package becomes empty, and GAR drops the package container from `ListPackages`. For multi-platform pushes (`docker buildx --push`, `skopeo copy --all`), the tag points to an OCI image index referencing N per-platform child manifests; DELETE removes only the index, leaving the N children as untagged orphan content. The package container persists in `ListPackages` because it still holds those untagged child manifests.

/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSPV-PodmanVmSupplyChain.adoc:166:  *byte-identical* digest fidelity. skopeo is confirmed out, but on a *different
/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSPV-PodmanVmSupplyChain.adoc:34:VM images are OCI artifacts rather than standard container images. They contain compressed root filesystem tarballs for WSL environments or CoreOS disk images for standard virtualization platforms. These artifacts use non-standard media types, rely on custom annotations such as disktype to indicate their intended use, and carry opaque blobs rather than composable filesystem layers. While they are platform-indexed in the OCI sense, they are not platform-runnable as containers would be. Because of these differences, standard `podman pull` and `docker pull` cannot correctly retrieve and reassemble VM artifacts — they operate at the container-runtime abstraction level, which assumes runnable images with standard layer structure. Tools like crane (manifest and blob-level registry operations), skopeo (cross-registry copying with format awareness), and oras (OCI Registry As Storage, designed for non-container artifacts) work below that abstraction at the registry API and manifest level, which is why they can handle VM artifacts correctly.
/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSPV-PodmanVmSupplyChain.adoc:36:The bootstrap process faces a chicken-and-egg problem. The mirroring workflow requires crane, oras, or skopeo, but Recipe Bottle does not want to obligate users to install and maintain these tools on their host systems. The solution is to use an ignite VM, initialized with the latest uncontrolled upstream image, as a disposable tooling environment. Crane and jq are installed inside the ignite VM, and all registry operations execute there. The ignite VM is discarded after completing the mirroring operation.

content.content:
1	==== Multi-Architecture Strategy Caveat
2	
3	All sections below assume Google Cloud Build (GCB) remains the sole builder.
4	GCB provides only x86 workers — default and private pools alike.
5	Multi-platform builds use QEMU emulation via the binfmt setup step (rbgjb02).
6	The binfmt step is conditionally included: vessels whose `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS` matches the build runner platform (`RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM`) skip it, reducing attack surface for native-only builds.
7	
8	If a vessel requires native ARM builds, the builder strategy must expand beyond GCB, and further GCB-specific hardening should be re-evaluated.
9	
10	If native ARM builds become necessary, builder-agnostic controls (cosign signing, portable SLSA provenance) become more valuable than GCB-specific infrastructure (private pools, VPC Service Controls).
11	See cosign deferral rationale in Deferred Items.
12	
13	==== Current Posture
14	
15	Properties of the build pipeline as of 2026-03.
16	Authoritative procedures live in the operational specs (RBSAC, RBSAV, RBSDC, RBSDN);
17	this section states what holds and why it matters.
18	
19	**Build definitions are fully static.**
20	Each vessel's `cloudbuild.json` is stitched from step scripts (`rbgjb*.sh`) and current regime pins -- zero custom substitution overrides at submission time.
21	The build is fully determined by the stitched JSON and the pouch (build context as OCI image in GAR).
22	See RBSAC.
23	
24	**SLSA v1.0 Build Level 3 provenance — verified.**
25	All `builds.create` submissions produce SLSA v1.0 Build Level 3 provenance via `requestedVerifyOption: VERIFIED` and the `images:` field declaring per-platform image URIs.
26	Google Container Analysis generates both v0.1 and v1 predicate attestations per image.
27	Provenance chain: stitched JSON + pouch -> per-platform image digest.
28	Verification via DSSE envelope signature verification (jq + openssl against embedded GCB attestor keys).
29	
30	Production-verified build IDs:
31	
32	* Single-arch: `16d3b60f` (busybox-amd64), `fc36b970` (busybox-arm64), `9180c42a` (trbim-macos)
33	* Multi-platform: `2e172ce0` (rbev-busybox, 3 platforms — amd64, arm64, armv7)
34	
35	**Security boundary: pouch and stitched JSON.**
36	Cloud Build receives build context via the pouch (FROM SCRATCH OCI image in GAR) -- per-vessel Dockerfile and context files.
37	The main repo (tooling, specs, regime files) is never exposed to Google.
38	The Director controls what enters the pouch; the cloud is just labor.
39	See RBSAC.
40	
41	**Builder step images are reliquary-sourced.**
42	All GCB step images are pulled from a datestamped reliquary in GAR — co-versioned tool images frozen by the inscribe ceremony.
43	`RBRV_RELIQUARY` in each vessel regime identifies the active reliquary.
44	Base images are separately enshrined via `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR`.
45	
46	**Supply-chain pinning boundary — staged on the reliquary.**
47	The governing rule: reliquary _generation_ (the inscribe/conclave ceremony that mints the sealed tool cohort) may pull unpinned upstream images — bootstrapping the first tool must start somewhere, and generation is the only phase permitted to.
48	Every build that _consumes_ a sealed reliquary carries zero unpinned aspects: each tool image resolves, pinned, from the cohort, whose durable home is our own Artifact Registry (GAR), never an upstream registry.
49	The made side already conforms — all conjure/vouch step images are reliquary-sourced (above).
50	The capture family is brought into conformance by the crane-embrace cutover: the capture image tool (gcrane) becomes a reliquary cohort member, so a sealed-reliquary capture (bole/wsl/podvm) resolves a _pinned_ gcrane, leaving the floating Google-hosted bootstrap gcrane (`ZRBLD_GCRANE_BUILDER`) only in conclave — which builds the reliquary and therefore cannot resolve from it.
51	Until that lands, capture builders ride floating bootstrap tags (see Deferred Items).
52	
53	**Dual private pool workers (tether + airgap).**
54	Every depot gets two private pools: a tether pool (public egress) and an airgap pool (`NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS`).
55	Pool routing is operation-level: inscribe and enshrine use tether (upstream access required), about and vouch always use airgap (verification isolated from public registries), conjure and bind use the vessel's `RBRV_EGRESS_MODE`.
56	Pools are fully managed by Google (scale to zero, no VMs to maintain).
57	
58	**Artifact Registry storage.**
59	Built images are stored in Google Artifact Registry (GAR), not Docker Hub or other external registries.
60	Regime variables configure project, region, and repository.
61	
62	**Dedicated Mason service account.**
63	Scoped to depot-project permissions, specified in the `serviceAccount` field of each build JSON.
64	
65	**SBOM generation.**
66	Per-platform Syft scans via `registry:` transport (reads directly from GAR using an OAuth2 token obtained from the GCB metadata server — no Docker daemon coupling).
67	Each platform image gets its own JSON and table-format SBOM, packaged into the per-platform metadata container.
68	Aligned with CISA 2025 minimum elements and EU CRA requirements.
69	The upgrade path is runtime-aware analysis, not removal.
70	
71	**Cloud Build logging.**
72	Build requests set `options.logging: CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY`.
73	Log hygiene (no credential echo) was audited during trigger migration script inlining.
74	
75	**Registry authentication is ambient and per-step, with no credential-helper dependency.**
76	Docker-based steps authenticate to GAR automatically — Cloud Build pre-populates Docker credentials, so no `docker login` step is needed.
77	The capture steps use **`gcrane`** (crane's Google-auth sibling — same `cp`/`manifest`/`tag` engine), whose `google.Keychain` matches `*.pkg.dev` and draws credentials from Application Default Credentials → the GCE metadata server.
78	On a Cloud Build worker the Mason SA *is* that metadata-server identity, so gcrane reads and writes GAR ambiently with **no token fetch, no `crane auth login`, no credential-helper image, and no new IAM grant**.
79	This is the same ambient model the docker-based steps already use, extended from docker to crane — one auth story across the capture family rather than two.
80	The one remaining tool that cannot auth ambiently is **skopeo** (the made-side bind mirror): skopeo cannot consume the gcloud credential helper — gcloud is not a single binary to place on the skopeo container's PATH — so each skopeo step fetches a short-lived OAuth2 token directly from the GCB metadata server (the Mason SA's ambient identity) and passes it via `--src-creds`/`--dest-creds`.
81	This is the canonical skopeo→Artifact Registry pattern, not a workaround — the credential-helper alternative was evaluated and rejected (see Deferred Items).
82	The token is held in step-local memory and re-minted by each step that needs it; it is never written to `/workspace`.
83	_Invariant: the {rbtgr_workspace} carries non-secret build data only — secrets stay step-local._
84	As skopeo is evicted across the crane-embrace cutovers, the per-step token fetch retires entirely and ambient gcrane auth becomes universal.
85	
86	**Provenance on demand.**
87	`rbfl_tally()` lists all hallmarks and queries Container Analysis inline for each per-platform image, displaying SLSA build level, build invocation ID, and single-origin verification per hallmark.
88	Tabtarget:
89	{rbtgo_hallmark_tally}.
90	
91	===== Pipeline Architecture: Single-Platform Vessels
92	
93	Five build steps plus CB-native `images:` push (binfmt step excluded at stitch time).
94	Applies to vessels whose `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS` matches `RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM`.
95	
96	1. **derive-tag-base** — compute timestamps and tag components from CB substitutions; log build strategy
97	2. **build-and-load** — `docker buildx build --load` (single platform into local daemon)
98	3. **sbom** — Syft scan via `registry:` transport
99	4. **assemble-metadata** — per-platform `build_info.json` with SLSA summary fields
100	5. **build-and-push-metadata** — multi-platform `about` container pushed to `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/about:<hallmark>` via `buildx --push`
101	
102	`images:` field declares the single per-platform URI → CB pushes and generates SLSA Level 3.
103	
104	===== Pipeline Architecture: Multi-Platform Vessels
105	
106	Seven build steps plus CB-native `images:` push (binfmt step included at stitch time).
107	Applies to vessels whose `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS` differs from `RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM`.
108	About steps (`rbgja/`) are embedded in the build job via stitch (combined image+about).
109	Vouch (`rbgjv/`) runs as a separate Cloud Build job.
110	
111	1. **derive-tag-base** — compute timestamps and tag components; log build strategy
112	2. **qemu-binfmt** — register QEMU user-static for cross-arch emulation
113	3. **buildx-push-multi** — `docker buildx build --push` all platforms to intermediate `-multi` tag; capture `cache_before.json` (Docker daemon state pre-build) and `buildkit_metadata.json` (via `--metadata-file`)
114	4. **per-platform-pullback** — `docker pull --platform` each arch, tag individually
115	5. **push-per-platform** — `docker push` each per-platform tag (pre-push for imagetools); capture `cache_after.json` (Docker daemon state post-push)
116	6. **imagetools-create** — reassemble consumer-facing manifest list from per-platform tags
117	7. **push-diags** — build `FROM scratch` container with build-time diagnostic files (`buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json`, `recipe.txt`), push as `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/diags:«HALLMARK»`
118	
119	`images:` field declares all per-platform URIs → CB re-pushes (idempotent) and generates SLSA Level 3.
120	The reassembled manifest list provides transparent platform resolution to attested per-platform images.

131	];
132	
133	/// Declared dependencies — the RBS0 Dependency Inventory (consumer + developer
134	/// + specialized). A cost accepted by every consumer; each appears in RBS0 with
135	/// its justification.
136	pub(crate) const ZRBTDRU_DECLARED_DEPS: &[&str] = &[
137	    "bash", "cargo", "curl", "docker", "git", "jq", "openssl", "podman", "scp",
138	    "shellcheck", "ssh", "ssh-keygen", "stat", "tar", "tee", "timeout",
139	];
140	
141	/// Curated GCB container-tool allowlist — the external commands present in the
142	/// controlled builder images (alpine/docker→busybox, gcloud→Debian, skopeo→Fedora,
143	/// gcrane:debug→busybox, the wsl-underpin fetch step→Debian) that GCB-bash
144	/// legitimately uses, enumerated empirically via the cupel inventory over
145	/// Tools/rbk/rbgj*. GCB-allowed = POSIX floor (universal in every container) ∪
146	/// this list. It deliberately does NOT inherit the kit declared deps: membership
147	/// is per-container-presence, not portability. Each entry is empirically present
148	/// in the controlled builder that uses it — openssl/gpg/apt-get on the Debian
149	/// wsl-underpin fetch builder, gcrane/tar in gcrane:debug busybox — while jq is
150	/// still absent everywhere and the rbgj* scripts avoid it by hand ("no jq
151	/// dependency — use grep+cut"), so a GCB script reaching for a tool outside its
152	/// controlled container still fails — a supply-chain conformance check, not
153	/// merely a portability one.
154	pub(crate) const ZRBTDRU_GCB_ALLOWED: &[&str] = &[
155	    "apt-get", "awk", "cat", "curl", "cut", "docker", "gcrane", "gpg",
156	    "grep", "head", "ls", "openssl", "sha256sum", "shasum", "skopeo",
157	    "tar", "tr", "wget",
158	];
159	
160	/// Bash builtins and command-position keywords that name no external command —
161	/// never flagged. Keywords with control-flow semantics ([[, ]], the loop/branch
162	/// words) are handled structurally in the lexer; the rest live here so that a
163	/// builtin reaching classification (echo, printf, read, local, …) is cleared.
164	pub(crate) const ZRBTDRU_BUILTINS: &[&str] = &[
165	    ":", ".", "[", "[[", "]", "]]", "alias", "bg", "bind", "break", "builtin",
166	    "caller", "cd", "command", "compgen", "complete", "compopt", "continue",
167	    "coproc", "declare", "dirs", "disown", "echo", "enable", "eval", "exec",
168	    "exit", "export", "false", "fc", "fg", "getopts", "hash", "help", "history",
169	    "jobs", "kill", "let", "local", "logout", "mapfile", "popd", "printf",
170	    "pushd", "pwd", "read", "readarray", "readonly", "return", "set", "shift",
171	    "shopt", "source", "suspend", "test", "times", "trap", "true", "type",
172	    "typeset", "ulimit", "umask", "unalias", "unset", "wait",
173	];
174	
175	/// One evicted command and the BCG-prescribed replacement reported in its
176	/// stead. Verbatim from BCG's "Evicted Utilities" table.
177	pub(crate) struct zrbtdru_Eviction {
178	    pub(crate) command: &'static str,
179	    pub(crate) replacement: &'static str,
180	}
181	
182	/// The enforced eviction table. In kit-bash these fail with the replacement; in
183	/// GCB-bash they are tolerated (the cloud-sdk image carries them and the
184	/// portability concern is moot).
185	pub(crate) const ZRBTDRU_EVICTIONS: &[zrbtdru_Eviction] = &[

1222	######################################################################
1223	# Mirror (bind vessel → GAR)
1224	
1225	rbfd_mirror() {
1226	  zrbfd_sentinel
1227	
1228	  local z_vessel_dir="${1:-}"
1229	
1230	  # Documentation block
1231	  buc_doc_brief "Mirror a bind vessel image from upstream to GAR via combined Cloud Build (skopeo copy + about)"
1232	  buc_doc_param "vessel_dir" "Path to vessel directory containing rbrv.env"
1233	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
1234	
1235	  # No-arg: list available vessels
1236	  if test -z "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
1237	    local z_sigils
1238	    z_sigils=$(rbrv_list_capture) || buc_die "No vessels found"
1239	    buc_step "Available vessels:"
1240	    local z_sigil=""
1241	    for z_sigil in ${z_sigils}; do
1242	      buc_bare "        ${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_sigil}"
1243	    done
1244	    buc_die "Vessel directory required"
1245	  fi
1246	
1247	  # Load and validate vessel
1248	  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
1249	  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-}" = "rbnve_bind" \
1250	    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' is not a bind vessel (mode: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-unset})"
1251	  test -n "${RBRV_BIND_IMAGE:-}" \
1252	    || buc_die "RBRV_BIND_IMAGE not set for bind vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}'"
1253	
1254	  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (mirror uses skopeo + about steps from reliquary)
1255	  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images
1256	
1257	  # Dirty-tree guard — mirror stamps HEAD into the about metadata and composes
1258	  # its cloud step bodies from the working tree; both must match a commit.
1259	  bug_require_clean_tree "mirroring"
1260	
1261	  # Authenticate as Director
1262	  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
1263	  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"
1264	
1265	  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
1266	  local z_token
1267	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
1268	    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"
1269	
1270	  # Registry preflight -- verify reliquary and base images exist before expensive operations
1271	  zrbfd_registry_preflight "${z_token}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
1272	
1273	  # GAR coordinates
1274	  local -r z_gar_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
1275	  local -r z_gar_base="${z_gar_host}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
1276	
1277	  # Generate hallmark timestamps: bYYMMDDHHMMSS-rYYMMDDHHMMSS

1279	  local -r z_build_ts_file="${ZRBFD_MIRROR_PREFIX}build_ts.txt"

190	===== OCI Layout Bridge — Superseded
191	
192	The original OCI Layout Bridge (RBSOB) — export to OCI tarball, push via crane, split with skopeo — is fully superseded for all vessel types.
193	Single-platform vessels use `buildx --load` into the local daemon.
194	Multi-platform vessels use `buildx --push` with per-platform pullback.
195	CB pre-populates GAR credentials; no explicit docker login step is needed.
196	See RBSOB superseded notice.
197	
198	==== Implemented: Egress Lockdown via Dual Pools
199	
200	Egress lockdown is implemented through the dual-pool architecture.
201	The airgap pool (`NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS`) blocks all public internet access.
202	Operations that require upstream access (inscribe, enshrine) run on the tether pool.
203	Operations that must be isolated (about, vouch) always run on the airgap pool.
204	Conjure and bind are routed per vessel's `RBRV_EGRESS_MODE`.
205	
206	**Dependency mirroring is universal.**
207	All GCB step images come from reliquaries (inscribed tool images in GAR).
208	All base images come from anchors (enshrined base images in GAR).
209	This is not just an airgap feature — it is a universal robustness measure against upstream registry churn.
210	
211	**Operation pool routing:**
212	
213	[cols="1,1,2",options="header"]
214	|===
215	|Operation
216	|Pool
217	|Rationale
218	
219	|Inscribe
220	|tether
221	|Pulls tool images from upstream registries
222	
223	|Enshrine
224	|tether
225	|Pulls base images from upstream via skopeo
226	
227	|Conjure/Bind
228	|vessel's RBRV_EGRESS_MODE
229	|Build-time deps vary per vessel
230	
231	|About
232	|airgap (always)
233	|Only needs GAR + Google APIs (Private Google Access)
234	
235	|Vouch
236	|airgap (always)
237	|Verification isolated from public registries
238	|===
239	
240	**About/vouch always-airgap is a security property**: verification can never be influenced by a compromised public registry, even for tether vessels.
241	
242	==== Implemented: Cloud-Dispatched Tool-Plane Deletes
243	
244	GAR image-package deletes — `banish` (whole Lode) and `abjure` (every package under a hallmark subtree) — are **cloud-dispatched**, not host-issued.
245	The workstation submits a Cloud Build and blocks until terminal (conjure-shaped); the in-pool step deletes each package by convergence (see *Delete-by-convergence* below) and verifies package absence.
246	The build's outcome IS the delete outcome, which closes a real correctness gap: a host-issued `packages.delete` returns HTTP 200 immediately and the prior code trusted that 200, but the actual deletion runs asynchronously as an LRO whose terminal verdict the workstation never saw (the trust-200 LRO gap).
247	
248	**The cinched line is tool-plane versus control-plane.**
249	Only GAR *package* deletes move cloud-side: `banish` and `abjure`.
250	Control-plane deletes — service accounts, projects, the depot, liens, GCS buckets — stay host-issued REST, as do the member/tag-grain maintenance verbs (the raw image backdoor), which must not depend on the pipeline they exist to clean up after.
251	The recorded canon is the line itself, not a trajectory toward all-deletes-go-cloud.
252	
253	**The delete build runs as Director, not Mason.**
254	Director already holds `repoAdmin` (delete); Mason is writer-only and stays that way, so the identity that executes untrusted upstream capture bytes is never widened with delete authority.
255	Running a build *as* the Director SA, submitted by the Director-authenticated workstation, requires one investiture addition: Director holds `serviceAccountUser` (actAs) on *itself* — the self-actAs binding that lets a SA run a build as itself — alongside the `workerPoolUser` and Cloud Build Editor grants it already carries.
256	No new GAR grant is introduced.
257	
258	**Delete-by-convergence at the vendor Palisade.**
259	GAR refuses to delete a child manifest while its parent index still exists: a `versions.delete` (or a whole-`packages.delete`) on a referenced child returns FAILED_PRECONDITION, "manifest is referenced by parent manifests", and a single `packages.delete` of a multi-arch web removes nothing — the package survives intact.
260	This is GAR's documented behavior — its cleanup policies "don't delete images referenced by a parent manifest; if the parent manifest is deleted, then any related images are deleted" on the next run — and the reason Google's own gcr-cleaner ships a `--skip-errors` flag rather than ordering the topology.
261	The in-pool step does the same: each round it fires a delete at every remaining version (force=true to drop tags) and at the package shell, skipping the per-round "referenced by parent" preconditions, and polls the package GET until it 404s.
262	The parent indexes delete first (they have no parent), freeing their children for a later round; absence is the only success signal, the deadline the only failsafe — the same eventual-consistency shape as the host `rbuh_poll_until_gone`.
263	This is conduct at the vendor Palisade: an empirical probe (2026-06-09) recorded the FAILED_PRECONDITION signature directly — the earlier paddock guess of an LRO terminating NOT_FOUND (code 5) "even when the delete effectively completed" was wrong; the package does not delete at all under a single call, and the step converges on the observable end state rather than trusting any LRO verdict.
264	Multi-arch capture (bole/wsl keep full-fidelity index Lodes) keeps index-web packages a live shape, so the convergence loop is load-bearing.
265	
266	**Accepted cost: every fixture banish/abjure becomes a build.**
267	Cloud dispatch moves these deletes from a sub-second host REST call to a full Cloud Build (minutes, serialized with other fixture builds).
268	This is accepted as the price of delete correctness; the depot is disposable, so debris is test material, not cleanup duty.
269	
270	==== Potential Hardening: VPC Service Controls Perimeter
271	
272	Wrap the depot project in a VPC Service Controls (VPC-SC) perimeter.
273	This restricts which GCP APIs can be called and from where, reducing data exfiltration risk.
274	
275	**Required infrastructure:**
276	
277	* VPC-SC perimeter around the depot project (organizational policy — may require org-level admin)
278	* Access levels for operator workstations (so builds can still be submitted from outside the perimeter)
279	* Ingress/egress rules for the perimeter (which services may cross the boundary)
280	* Testing: every GCP API call in the pipeline must be verified inside the perimeter — Cloud Logging writes, GCS uploads, Artifact Registry pushes, Cloud Build API calls can break subtly
281	
282	**Prerequisite:** Egress Lockdown is in place (dual-pool architecture).
283	
284	==== Deferred Items
285	
286	Evaluated and consciously deferred; each has a specific revisit trigger.
287	
288	**Dockerfile base image digest validation.**
289	Builder step images are mostly pinned (reliquary tools ride a content-frozen cohort stamp); the always-pullable Google-hosted bootstrap builders (`gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker`, `gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug`) ride floating tags by design, and user-authored Dockerfiles in vessel build contexts are not validated.
290	A vessel with `FROM ubuntu:22.04` passes today — mutable tag, supply-chain risk.
291	This constrains vessel authors (policy decision with RBSRV implications), orthogonal to pipeline infrastructure.
292	Separate future heat.
293	
294	**Bootstrap builder pinning + gcr.io provenance — recorded decision (2026-06).**
295	The reliquary's own generators cannot resolve their tools from the reliquary (a cycle), so a small set of bootstrap builders is pulled unpinned: `gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker` and `gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug`.
296	This is acceptable because it is bounded to reliquary _generation_ (see _Supply-chain pinning boundary_, Current Posture) — sealed-reliquary builds never touch these.
297	On gcr.io durability: the Container Registry _storage product_ shut down (writes 2025-03-18, reads 2025-06-03), but Google-_owned_ images served via gcr.io URLs (cloud-builders, go-containerregistry, distroless) were migrated onto Artifact Registry under the preserved gcr.io URL and are explicitly exempt, with no announced end-of-life.
298	RB stores nothing at gcr.io — every RB artifact lives in Artifact Registry — so the Container Registry shutdown does not touch RB's foundation; the only gcr.io exposure is these bootstrap-generation pulls, which are non-load-bearing (rare, supervised, re-runnable; a one-line source change if an alias is ever retired).
299	Hardening (sibling itch, not yet scoped): digest-pin the bootstrap builders, or mirror them into our Artifact Registry for full control — the durable answer the reliquary already embodies for consumed tools.
300	See _Registry transition_ references below.
301	
302	**BuildKit provenance flag.**
303	`--provenance=true` on `docker buildx build` records builder internals — useful for deeper forensics.
304	GCB-native SLSA v1.0 provenance covers the primary attestation use case.
305	Redundant for the current threat model.
306	Revisit if build reproducibility verification becomes a priority.
307	
308	**Cosign image signing.**
309	Cryptographic signatures attached to images, verifiable by downstream consumers.
310	Two modes: keyless (Sigstore/Fulcio — requires public internet, conflicts with Egress Lockdown) and keyed (Cloud KMS — no external dependency).
311	With CB-native SLSA v1.0 Build Level 3 verified on all vessel types, cosign is redundant for the current threat model (building and consuming within own GCP project).
312	Becomes valuable if: images are distributed externally, trust verification needed outside GCP, or builder strategy expands beyond GCB (losing Google's provenance).
313	If adopted, prefer KMS-backed keyed signing (`cosign sign --key gcpkms://...`) for Egress Lockdown compatibility.
314	
315	**Binary Authorization.**
316	Deploy-time policy enforcement: only attested images can be deployed.
317	Relevant for GKE and Cloud Run.
318	Now technically achievable — CB-native SLSA Level 3 satisfies BinAuth attestation requirements.
319	Recipe Bottle deploys to Podman on workstations — no benefit until deployment targets change.
320	
321	**Reproducibility verification.**
322	Periodic independent rebuilds compared by digest.
323	Requires reproducible toolchains — a vessel-author discipline, not a pipeline control.
324	Not actionable until vessel ecosystem matures.
325	
326	**skopeo credential helper — rejected, not merely deferred (evaluated 2026-06).**
327	The standalone `docker-credential-gcr` helper could let skopeo authenticate to GAR via a `credHelpers` config entry instead of the inline token fetch (see Current Posture).
328	Rejected: on GCB the helper mints the _same_ metadata-server token we already obtain (no new capability), but requires injecting a new binary into the skopeo container — a fresh vouched supply-chain dependency, the opposite of our minimize-the-dependency-manifold posture — and skopeo→GAR via credential helper carries documented 400-error friction (skopeo issue #2573, locked/unresolved), with the community fallback being the explicit `oauth2accesstoken` token, i.e. exactly the current approach.
329	Revisit only if skopeo gains first-class GAR/ADC authentication or the helper friction is resolved upstream.
330	Scope note: the capture steps no longer use skopeo at all — the crane-embrace moved them to gcrane, which auths GAR ambiently (see Current Posture) — so this rejection now governs only the remaining made-side skopeo (the bind mirror) until it too is evicted.
331	
332	**Capture-machinery duplication — token fetch, skopeo-capture, vouch-push.**
333	The cloud capture steps presently duplicate logic by copy: the metadata-token fetch across the remaining skopeo steps, the read→digest→fingerprint→copy block (the enshrine fork — bole now rides the shared `gcrane-fingerprint` snippet and gcrane's ambient auth), and the `FROM scratch` vouch-push step (Lode `rbgjl02` and hallmark-verify `rbgjv03`).
334	This is acknowledged, not accidental — Cloud Build offers no native step reuse (no YAML anchors or includes), and its blessed reuse mechanism, custom builder images, conflicts with the _cloud is just labor; the Director controls what enters_ posture above (a custom builder holding our logic would be a new vouched artifact rather than inline-from-repo).
335	Remedy (built in the capture-machinery-unification refactor): a host assembly spine (aligned with the `stitch` vocabulary), a shared cloud step-library (`rbgjs`) of compose-once snippets, and a dispatch-time recipe validator that derives each step's substitution coverage — composed once and referenced, replacing copy-by-example with compose-from-library. The `/workspace` inter-step channel stays guarded in-step (the steps' own `test -f` preconditions) rather than by a dispatch contract.
336	Dedup is deliberately partial: only the survivor (bole) rides the spine; the doomed enshrine and inscribe forks are left in place for later removal, so the duplicated copies above persist until that cutover.
337	Behavior-preservation is confirmed against the live system at the heat's milestone skirmish gate (full skirmish + service).
338

237	*The library (each snippet's requires/provides):*
238	
239	[cols="1,3",options="header"]
240	|===
241	|Snippet |Contract
242	
243	|`token-fetch`        |_requires_ none (ambient metadata server); _provides_ `TOKEN`. The canonical skopeo-to-GAR metadata-server token (RBSCB), in-memory, never to `/workspace`.
244	|`gcrane-fingerprint` |_requires_ `ORIGIN`, `RAW_FILE`; _provides_ `SHA`, `FINGERPRINT`. Read the raw manifest (`gcrane manifest`, registry bytes verbatim), take its canonical sha256, derive the sanitized-origin glance fingerprint.
245	|`buildx-bootstrap`   |_requires_ none; _provides_ the `rb-builder` buildx builder, created-if-absent and selected. Idempotent under retry; run once per step.
246	|`buildx-push`        |_requires_ `PUSH_URI`, `PUSH_PLATFORMS`, `PUSH_CTX` (and the bootstrap builder); _provides_ the image pushed to `PUSH_URI`. The irreducible FROM-scratch push.
247	|===
248	
249	*Share the primitive, not the whole step.* The vouch-push step (Lode `rbgjl02`,
250	hallmark-verify `rbgjv03`) is NOT one shared step: the two diverge on five
251	load-bearing axes — push cardinality (loop-N members vs single), destination-URI
252	template and arity, platform source (literal `linux/amd64` vs a `/workspace`
253	platforms file), a conditional Dockerfile (conjure verify files), and disjoint
254	substitution sets. Only the buildx bootstrap and the push invocation are
255	identical, so only those are shared — as `buildx-bootstrap` + `buildx-push`.
256	Cardinality, URI construction, platform choice, and context assembly (which JSON,
257	which Dockerfile) stay in the per-kind step. The same boundary governs
258	`gcrane-fingerprint`: the read→digest→fingerprint core is identical across
259	captures, but the first GAR copy is excluded because its destination tag is
260	itself digest-derived and kind-specific, so it cannot be a clean shared
261	parameter. The rule the library follows: share the irreducible identical core,
262	leave kind-specific assembly in the kind.
263	
264	*In-heat reach (partial dedup, forward payoff).* `buildx-bootstrap`/`buildx-push`
265	have two live callers (`rbgjl02`, `rbgjv03`). `gcrane-fingerprint` has one live
266	caller (bole's `rbgjl01`); the enshrine fork (`rbgje01`) keeps its own inline
267	read→digest→fingerprint copy until that cutover. `token-fetch` no longer has a
268	snippet caller at all — bole dropped it when it moved to gcrane's ambient auth,
269	and the only remaining metadata-token logic is the inline copy in the doomed
270	skopeo steps; the snippet retires when they are evicted. The snippets are
271	authored once now regardless: the library teaches "compose from the library,"
272	and the dedup completes when the doomed forks are removed.
273

input.content:
#!/busybox/sh
# RBGJM Step 01: Mirror image from upstream to GAR via gcrane
# Builder: gcrane (from reliquary — :debug variant carries /busybox/sh)
# Substitutions: _RBGA_GAR_HOST, _RBGA_GAR_PATH, _RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT,
#                _RBGA_HALLMARK, _RBGA_BIND_SOURCE
#
# Registry-to-registry copy preserving multi-platform manifest lists.
# gcrane authenticates GAR ambiently via its google.Keychain (Mason SA,
# GCE metadata server) — no token fetch needed.
#
# Destination uses canonical AAK shape:
#   ${gar_host}/${gar_path}/${HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${hallmark}/image:${hallmark}
# matching rbgja02 (syft), rbgja04 (about), rbgjv03 (vouch).

set -eu

test -n "${_RBGA_GAR_HOST}"        || { echo "_RBGA_GAR_HOST missing"        >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${_RBGA_GAR_PATH}"        || { echo "_RBGA_GAR_PATH missing"        >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${_RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT}"  || { echo "_RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT missing"  >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${_RBGA_HALLMARK}"        || { echo "_RBGA_HALLMARK missing"        >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE}"     || { echo "_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE missing"     >&2; exit 1; }

DEST_REF="${_RBGA_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGA_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${_RBGA_HALLMARK}/image:${_RBGA_HALLMARK}"

echo "=== Mirroring bind image via gcrane ==="
echo "Source: ${_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE}"
echo "Dest:   ${DEST_REF}"

# gcrane authenticates GAR ambiently through its google.Keychain
# (ADC -> GCE metadata server, the Mason SA). No --creds needed.
gcrane cp "${_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE}" "${DEST_REF}" \
  || { echo "gcrane cp failed" >&2; exit 1; }

echo "Image mirrored: ${DEST_REF}"
echo "=== Mirror step complete ==="

128	
129	- **python**: `json.dump` / `json.loads` — stdlib, free. Always.
130	- **host**: `jq -n --arg/--slurpfile` — the host has `jq`; never concatenate,
131	  never YAML (Cloud Build's wire format is JSON; RBSCJ).
132	- **bash, inside a step**: hand-rolled `printf`/concatenation, because pulling
133	  `jq` into a minimal skopeo container is friction — and permitted *only* because
134	  every interpolated value is controlled (hex digests, ISO timestamps, sanitized
135	  origins, SA emails), none of which can carry a literal quote:
136	
137	```bash
138	# No jq inside the step — values are controlled; none can carry a quote.
139	ENVELOPE='{'
140	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\","
141	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"tags\":[\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\",\"${FINGERPRINT}\"]"
142	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}"
143	```
144	
145	If a value could ever carry a quote or newline, it is not controlled — do not
146	hand-roll it.
147	
148	Key naming rides ACG's wire-format clause: JSON this project authors on both
149	sides carries the format's minted key-sprue (e.g. `rblv_*` for the Lode vouch
150	envelope); JSON conforming to a foreign schema keeps the foreign keys. Rule and
151	conversion move: ACG "wire formats are named homes" / ACGm_108.
152	
153	### Separate pure logic from network ops
154	
155	Resolve inputs, build paths, and author envelopes in a block with no network
156	calls; isolate `skopeo`/`gcloud`/`curl`/`urllib` calls in their own block. The
157	pure block is testable without the cloud; the network block can be stubbed.
158	BCG's visible-transformation philosophy applied to a step's internal structure.
159	
160	### Step-body skeleton
161	
162	**bash/sh**: a single file; first executable line `set -euo pipefail`; no module
163	header, kindle, or sentinel (BUK constructs absent in the builder). The shebang
164	is stamped by the assembler, not written by the author (CBh_101).
165	
166	**python**: stdlib only — no `pip install` in-step; the preamble above; `main()`
167	guarded by `if __name__ == "__main__":`.
168	
169	### GAR authentication idiom
170	
171	GAR reads/writes use the in-memory metadata token as credentials, never a
172	credential helper (the gcloud helper is unavailable to skopeo; rationale in
173	RBSCB). The token itself is fetched fresh per step — bash via the `token-fetch`
174	snippet (CBh_101), python via `urllib` against `metadata.google.internal`. skopeo
175	copies pass `--all` for multi-arch safety:
176	
177	```bash
178	skopeo copy --all "docker://${ORIGIN}" "docker://${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
179	  --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
180	  || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }
181	```
182	
183	The *invariant* that the token never reaches `/workspace` is CBi_102; this is the
184	*idiom* that honors it.
185	
186	---
187	
188	## Cited Rules (numbered — each has, or will have, a citer)
189	
190	Headers tag the rule: ❌ a failure mode, ✅ an established correct shape.
191	Families: `CBi_` cloud-step invariants (any language), `CBb_` bash/sh, `CBp_`
192	python, `CBh_` host-composition seam. Numbered from 101 to leave room for
193	insertions; once a rule has a citer it is never renumbered.
194	
195	### CBi — Cloud Step Invariants
196	
197	#### ✅ CBi_101: Substitutions are inputs — assert presence
198	
199	`_RBGx_*` values arrive as environment variables (submit-time expansion via
200	`automapSubstitutions`), not as arguments, and **may be empty**. Assert every
201	required input before use and die if absent; read optional inputs with an
202	explicit empty default and a documented absent-branch. The set a step requires is
203	declared in its header comment and enforced at dispatch (CBh_102).
204	
205	*Cited by:* step header contracts; the dispatch validator.
206	
207	#### ✅ CBi_102: The `/workspace` boundary — non-secret only, secrets step-local, guard every read
208	
209	`/workspace` is the inter-step bus. Write provenance envelopes, rosters, build
210	context — **never secrets**. Tokens and credentials stay step-local and
211	in-memory, re-minted per step (RBSCB records the canonical invariant). Every
212	consumer guards: the producing step may not have run or may have produced
213	nothing — test presence **and** non-emptiness, and die naming the missing
214	producer:
215	
216	```bash
217	test -f /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
218	  || { echo "FATAL: lode_stamps.txt not found — step 01 must run first" >&2; exit 1; }
219	test -s /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \

340	### CBh — Host-Composition Seam (what a body is authored against)
341	
342	The author writes the body, not the shebang, the Build JSON, or the dispatch.
343	These rules describe the machinery a body must fit.
344	
345	#### ✅ CBh_101: Shared bash logic is spliced on the host via `#@rbgjs_include`
346	
347	Cloud Build has no native step reuse, so shared **bash** logic is composed
348	*before submit*. A step marks an include point; the host expander
349	(`zrbfc_expand_includes`, `rbfcb_BuildHost.sh`) replaces each marker with the
350	body of `rbgjs/rbgjs-<name>.sh`, shebang stripped. A marker-free body is rewritten
351	unchanged (safe to call on every step); a missing snippet crashes the expander —
352	no silent skip.
353	
354	```bash
355	#@rbgjs_include token-fetch
356	#@rbgjs_include gcrane-fingerprint
357	```
358	
359	**Snippet contract** (RBSCJ): snippets read **plain shell vars** set before the
360	marker (CBb_102), not `_RBGx_` substitutions, and are idempotent (CBi_103). The
361	library shares only the irreducibly identical core; kind-specific assembly stays
362	in the step.
363	
364	| Snippet | Requires | Provides |
365	|---------|----------|----------|
366	| `token-fetch` | none (ambient metadata) | `TOKEN` |
367	| `gcrane-fingerprint` | `ORIGIN`, `RAW_FILE` | `SHA`, `FINGERPRINT` |
368	| `gcrane-append` | `APPEND_CTX`, `APPEND_URI` | the FROM-scratch single-layer image pushed to `APPEND_URI` |
369	| `gpg-verify-sums` | `SUMS_URL`, `SIG_URL`, `TARGET_BASENAME`, `KEY_FPR` | `EXPECTED_SHA` |
370	| `buildx-bootstrap` | none | the `rb-builder` buildx builder |
371	| `buildx-push` | `PUSH_URI`, `PUSH_PLATFORMS`, `PUSH_CTX` | the image pushed |
372	
373	Bash-only today — see CBp_101 for the python gap. The recipe row's `entrypoint`
374	field (`bash`/`sh`/`busybox`/`python3`) selects the shebang the spine stamps; the
375	author declares the interpreter there (`busybox` → `#!/busybox/sh`, the only shell
376	in a distroless `:debug` builder such as `gcrane:debug`).
377	
378	*Cited by:* every `#@rbgjs_include` site; the snippet contract.
379	
380	#### ✅ CBh_102: The substitution blob is opaque to the spine; coverage is validated at dispatch
381	
382	A per-kind body builds the substitution blob (`jq -n` → `_RBGx_` keys) and hands
383	it to the spine, which passes it through without reading any individual key
384	(keeping the spine kind-agnostic). Before the expensive submit, the validator
385	scans each include-expanded step body for the `_RBGx_` tokens it references
386	(*requires*) and fails if any is absent from the blob's keys (*provides*) — the
387	heat's one declared behavior-add, whose reject path carries its own unit tests.
388	A body author's obligation: every `_RBGx_` the body reads must be a key the
389	kind's blob provides.
390	
391	*Cited by:* the validator's reject message; its unit tests.
392	
393	#### ✅ CBh_103: A step returns data to the host via `buildStepOutputs`
394	
395	`/workspace` does not survive the build. A step that must hand a value back writes
396	it to the `buildStepOutputs` channel (`/builder/outputs/output`); the spine
397	extracts the slot and base64-decodes it host-side into a capture file. This is
398	the only step→host return path. The slot is addressed **by step index**
399	(`.results.buildStepOutputs[N]`) — N is the producing step's position in the
